items.title: Reproducibility Reports of the 2024 International Conference on Management of Data, SIGMOD ARI Reports 2024, Santiago, Chile, June 9-15, 2024
items.abstract:
This is the inaugural SIGMOD ARI Companion with Reproducibility Reports for SIGMOD papers that are fully reproduced following the ACM SIGMOD Availability and Reproducibility Initiative (ARI) process as outlined at https://reproducibility.sigmod.org/. The ACM SIGMOD 2024 Availability and Reproducibility Initiative requested from the authors of all papers presented in ACM SIGMOD 2024 to submit their artifacts for evaluation. The evaluation process may have a number of different outcomes. All papers for which the artifacts are made available received the "Artifacts Available" badge. Further, when the artifacts are found to be exercisable and well-documented, the corresponding papers received the "Artifacts Evaluated" badge. Finally, when the artifacts led to a full reproducibility of the presented results, the papers received the "Results Reproduced" badge. More details on the ACM badging can be found on the official ACM website: https://www.acm.org/publications/policies/artifact-review-and-badgingcurrent.

From a total of 213 paper presented in SIGMOD 2024, we received 72 artifact submissions, 43 of targeting the "Artifacts Available" and "Artifacts Evaluated" and 29 targeting the "Results Reproduced" badge as well. After a thorough reproducibility review, 24 papers were found fully reproducible and the reviewers compiled reproducibility reports for each of those papers. We highlight that not finding a paper fully reproduced is not a negative result, rather, it is a "not yet reproduced" result and the authors may decide to submit their artifacts for reproducibility review in a subsequent round.
items.abstract_zh:
这是首份 SIGMOD ARI 配套文件，包含 SIGMOD 论文的再现性报告，按照 https://reproducibility.sigmod.org/ 中概述的 ACM SIGMOD 可用性和再现性计划 (ARI) 流程进行了完全再现。 ACM SIGMOD 2024 可用性和再现性计划要求 ACM SIGMOD 2024 中发表的所有论文的作者提交他们的工件以供评估。评估过程可能会产生多种不同的结果。所有提供工件的论文都获得了“工件可用”徽章。此外，当发现文物可以使用并且有详细记录时，相应的论文就会获得“文物评估”徽章。最后，当这些工件能够完全再现所呈现的结果时，论文将获得“结果再现”徽章。有关 ACM 徽章的更多详细信息，请访问 ACM 官方网站：https://www.acm.org/publications/policies/artifact-review-and-badgingcurrent。

从 SIGMOD 2024 上提交的总共 213 篇论文中，我们收到了 72 份工件提交，其中 43 份针对“可用工件”和“已评估工件”，还有 29 份针对“结果再现”徽章。经过彻底的再现性审查后，发现 24 篇论文完全可再现，审稿人为每篇论文编制了再现性报告。我们强调，未找到完全复制的论文并不是负面结果，而是“尚未复制”的结果，作者可能决定在下一轮中提交他们的作品进行可复制性审查。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'ALP: Adaptive Lossless Floating-Point Compression'.
items.abstract: This report describes the reproducibility evaluation carried out for the research paper ''ALP: Adaptive Lossless Floating-Point Compression'', authored by Azim Afroozeh, Leonardo Kuffó, and Peter Boncz from the Centrum Wiskunde & Informatica (CWI), located in Amsterdam, Netherlands. The paper, presented at SIGMOD 2024, introduces ALP, a novel adaptive lossless compression algorithm for floating-point datasets. The results of the reproducibility evaluation confirm the core contributions of the original paper, showing that ALP consistently outperforms state-of-the-art techniques in both compression ratio and (de)compression speed.
items.abstract_zh: 本报告介绍了对研究论文“ALP：自适应无损浮点压缩”进行的可重复性评估，该论文由荷兰阿姆斯特丹 Centrum Wiskunde & Informatica (CWI) 的 Azim Afroozeh、Leonardo Kuffó 和 Peter Boncz 撰写。该论文在 SIGMOD 2024 上发表，介绍了 ALP，一种用于浮点数据集的新型自适应无损压缩算法。再现性评估的结果证实了原始论文的核心贡献，表明 ALP 在压缩比和（解）压缩速度方面始终优于最先进的技术。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'NOCAP: Near-Optimal Correlation-Aware Partitioning Joins'.
items.abstract: We have successfully replicated the key performance results of the paper depicted in Figure 8, despite not having access to the same experimental hardware. We thank the authors for providing resources for verifying the experiments and assisting with setup and debugging.
items.abstract_zh: 尽管无法访问相同的实验硬件，但我们已经成功复制了图 8 中所示论文的关键性能结果。我们感谢作者提供资源来验证实验并协助设置和调试。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'PECJ: Stream Window Join on Disorder Data Streams with Proactive Error Compensation'.
items.abstract: This paper proposes a new solution to handle the issues posed by out-of-order arrival of data when computing a stream window join (SWJ). The authors propose PECJ, a system which proactively manages disordered data streams by relying not just on already arrived data but also factoring in late-arriving data when updating the results of the join. The authors present a set of experiments comparing the efficacy of PECJ to state-of-the-art methods on a range of stream oscillation patterns. We find that on the hardware specified we were able to reproduce the results and plots shown in the paper, illustrating the robust nature of these experiments. Furthermore, the authors provided well-written scripts and easy-to-follow instructions to reproduce this material.
items.abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种新的解决方案来处理计算流窗口连接（SWJ）时数据无序到达所带来的问题。作者提出了 PECJ，这是一种主动管理无序数据流的系统，不仅依赖已到达的数据，而且在更新连接结果时还考虑迟到的数据。作者提出了一组实验，比较了 PECJ 与最先进的方法在一系列水流振荡模式上的功效。我们发现，在指定的硬件上，我们能够重现论文中显示的结果和图表，说明这些实验的稳健性。此外，作者还提供了编写良好的脚本和易于遵循的说明来复制此材料。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Dias: Dynamic Rewriting of Pandas Code'.
items.abstract: We successfully replicated all of the original paper's findings, including key performance results, as detailed in the experimental section. The authors provided data, scripts, and plotting tools to recreate key figures.
items.abstract_zh: 我们成功地复制了原始论文的所有发现，包括关键性能结果，如实验部分所述。作者提供了数据、脚本和绘图工具来重新创建关键人物。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'On Efficient Large Sparse Matrix Chain Multiplication'.
items.abstract: All major findings of the paper were reproduced. Unfortunately, due to privacy concerns, the authors could not share their code on GitHub.
items.abstract_zh: 论文的所有主要发现均被转载。不幸的是，出于隐私考虑，作者无法在 GitHub 上分享他们的代码。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Hierarchical Cut Labelling - Scaling Up Distance Queries on Road Networks'.
items.abstract: The core claims from the paper are reproducible. The authors provided a repository with the code needed to run the program, instructions on how to build and run, and links to the datasets to be used in the experiments.
items.abstract_zh: 论文的核心主张是可重复的。作者提供了一个存储库，其中包含运行程序所需的代码、如何构建和运行的说明以及实验中使用的数据集的链接。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'StarfishDB: A Query Execution Engine for Relational Probabilistic Programming'.
items.abstract: The core thesis of this paper could easily be reproduced in the original environment. The authors provided an almost fully automated set of scripts that automatically execute the experiments. The experimental data was automatically collected and plotted. The resulting figures have some minor deviations from the originals, but the overall trends remain the same, and hence they can be considered a good fit.
items.abstract_zh: 本文的核心论文可以很容易地在原始环境中复制。作者提供了一组几乎完全自动化的脚本来自动执行实验。自动收集并绘制实验数据。所得数据与原始数据有一些微小偏差，但总体趋势保持不变，因此可以认为它们是很好的拟合。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Can Learned Indexes be Built Efficiently? A Deep Dive into Sampling Trade-offs'.
items.abstract: In general, most of the main experiments in the paper ''Can Learned Indexes be Built Efficiently? A Deep Dive into Sampling Trade-offs'' can be successfully reproduced.
items.abstract_zh: 总的来说，论文《Can Learned Indexes be Efficiently》中的大部分主要实验都是这样的。可以成功地重现“深入研究采样权衡”。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Temporal JSON Keyword Search'.
items.abstract: This is the reproducibility report for the paper ''Temporal JSON Keyword Search'' by Dyreson et al. The code the authors provided is available on GitHub https://github.com/cdyreson/temporalHierarchicalKeywordSearch. We tested the code's correctness, data availability, and the reproducibility of the results. In particular, we aim to replicate the two key findings of the evaluation: the cost of the temporal functionality and the additional overhead of sequenced search compared to the consequences version. Generally, the results are consistent with the original paper. Overall, the reproduced results align closely with those reported in the paper, demonstrating that the study is reproducible.
items.abstract_zh: 这是 Dyreson 等人的论文“Temporal JSON Keyword Search”的重现性报告。作者提供的代码可在 GitHub https://github.com/cdyreson/temporalHierarchicalKeywordSearch 上找到。我们测试了代码的正确性、数据可用性和结果的可重复性。特别是，我们的目标是复制评估的两个关键发现：时间功能的成本以及与结果版本相比的排序搜索的额外开销。总体来说，结果与原论文一致。总体而言，重现的结果与论文中报告的结果非常一致，表明该研究是可重现的。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Fast Maximal Quasi-clique Enumeration: A Pruning and Branching Co-Design Approach'.
items.abstract: The core thesis of the paper has been successfully reproduced. The authors provided a set of scripts that can execute the experiments and generate the individual plots. The resulting figures were near identical to the submitted paper.
items.abstract_zh: 论文核心论文已成功转载。作者提供了一组可以执行实验并生成单独图的脚本。得出的数据与提交的论文几乎相同。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'PreVision: An Out-of-Core Matrix Computation System with Optimal Buffer Replacement'.
items.abstract: We report our experience in reproducing the benchmarks from the paper "PreVision: An Out-of-Core Matrix Computation System with Optimal Buffer Replacement"[1]. While we successfully reproduced only a portion of the benchmarks from [1], we consider the current results to be sufficient to validate the core thesis of the paper.
items.abstract_zh: 我们报告了我们在论文“PreVision: An Out-of-Core Matrix Computation System with Optimal Buffer Replacement”[1] 中重现基准的经验。虽然我们仅成功地重现了[1]中的部分基准，但我们认为当前的结果足以验证本文的核心论文。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Grafite: Taming Adversarial Queries with Optimal Range Filters'.
items.abstract: We have successfully reproduced the evaluation results in the paper. We appreciate that the authors have prepared detailed documents and automation scripts for reproducibility. Our reproduced results show similar trends and relationships between the alternatives compared and support the scientific contributions and claims of the original paper.
items.abstract_zh: 我们成功地重现了论文中的评估结果。我们感谢作者准备了详细的文档和自动化脚本以实现可重复性。我们复制的结果显示了比较的替代方案之间相似的趋势和关系，并支持原始论文的科学贡献和主张。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'CAFE: Towards Compact, Adaptive, and Fast Embedding for Large-scale Recommendation Models'.
items.abstract: We have been able to reproduce the original paper's findings partially. The authors provided the documentation with clear instructions on reproducing each experiment, from pre-processing to training. While most of the code can be run without any modifications, some scripts require manual changes to select particular datasets. Moreover, some experiments failed with an error or were not reproduced due to hardware limitations. All experiments with avazu and criteo datasets were reproduced. Additionally, HotSketch performance tests were successfully repeated.
items.abstract_zh: 我们已经能够部分重现原始论文的发现。作者为文档提供了关于重现每个实验（从预处理到训练）的清晰说明。虽然大多数代码无需任何修改即可运行，但某些脚本需要手动更改才能选择特定数据集。此外，一些实验因错误而失败或由于硬件限制而无法重现。再现了 avazu 和 criteo 数据集的所有实验。此外，HotSketch 性能测试也已成功重复。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Optimizing Distributed Protocols with Query Rewrites'.
items.abstract: The authors provided detailed descriptions for setting up the re- quired system and performing experiments using individual scripts provided. While the experiments required considerable effort and time, the detailed instructions facilitated evaluating the results and generating graphs that reproduced the same patterns claimed by the authors in the paper Optimizing Distributed Protocols with Query Rewrites
items.abstract_zh: 作者提供了设置所需系统和使用提供的单独脚本进行实验的详细描述。虽然实验需要大量的精力和时间，但详细的说明有助于评估结果并生成图表，这些图表再现了作者在论文《使用查询重写优化分布式协议》中声称的相同模式

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'A Unified Approach for Resilience and Causal Responsibility with Integer Linear Programming (ILP) and LP Relaxations'.
items.abstract: We successfully reproduced the experimental results presented in the original paper. The authors provided clear documentation of the software and hardware requirements, along with well-structured scripts and datasets hosted in an organized GitHub repository. In this reproducibility report, we compare the original results with our reproduced findings, both of which show similar trends, thereby supporting the authors' claims.
items.abstract_zh: 我们成功地重现了原始论文中提出的实验结果。作者提供了有关软件和硬件要求的清晰文档，以及托管在有组织的 GitHub 存储库中的结构良好的脚本和数据集。在这份再现性报告中，我们将原始结果与再现的结果进行了比较，两者都显示出相似的趋势，从而支持了作者的主张。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Faster Algorithms for Fair Max-Min Diversification in Rd'.
items.abstract: This report evaluates the reproducibility of the paper titled ''Faster Algorithms for Fair Max-Min Diversification in R d ''. The paper's artifacts were available and evaluatable. Reproducibility was straightforward. The paper's core claims were reproduced, with minor variations in a few graphs.
items.abstract_zh: 本报告评估了题为“R d 中公平最大最小多样化的更快算法”的论文的可重复性。该论文的成果是可用且可评估的。再现性很简单。该论文的核心主张被复制，仅在一些图表中略有变化。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'PimPam: Efficient Graph Pattern Matching on Real Processing-in-Memory Hardware'.
items.abstract: This is a reproducibility report for the paper ''PimPam: Efficient Graph Pattern Matching on Real Processing-in-Memory Hardware'' published in SIGMOD 2024. The paper proposes an efficient graph pattern matching framework that utilizes the real Processing-in-Memory hardware UPMEM. The main evaluation results of the paper are successfully reproduced. They demonstrate the better performance of PimPam over conventional systems.
items.abstract_zh: 这是 SIGMOD 2024 上发表的论文“PimPam：在真实内存处理硬件上的高效图形模式匹配”的可重复性报告。该论文提出了一种利用真实内存处理硬件 UPMEM 的高效图形模式匹配框架。论文主要评价结果成功复现。他们展示了 PimPam 比传统系统更好的性能。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2023 Paper: 'MorphStream: Adaptive Scheduling for Scalable Transactional Stream Processing on Multicores'.
items.abstract: We were able to reproduce the findings of the original paper. This includes performance comparisons with other systems, measurements of throughput and latency for different workloads and scheduling strategies, handling of abort decisions, and scalability comparisons. In total, this involves sixteen different figures in the original paper that were reproduced.
items.abstract_zh: 我们能够重现原始论文的研究结果。这包括与其他系统的性能比较、不同工作负载和调度策略的吞吐量和延迟测量、中止决策的处理以及可扩展性比较。总共，这涉及复制的原始论文中的十六个不同的数字。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'FACET: Robust Counterfactual Explanation Analytics'.
items.abstract: We are able to faithfully reproduce the original paper's findings as well as the key results reported in its experimental section (i.e., explanation quality, speed, robustness). The authors provided example data, comprehensive scripts, and plotting functions that allowed near-identical reconstruction of all of the paper's figures.
items.abstract_zh: 我们能够忠实地重现原始论文的发现以及实验部分报告的关键结果（即解释质量、速度、稳健性）。作者提供了示例数据、综合脚本和绘图函数，可以对论文中的所有图形进行近乎相同的重建。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'DProvDB: Differentially Private Query Processing with Multi-Analyst Provenance'.
items.abstract: The paper [1] introduces DProvDB, a fine-grained privacy provenance framework. Our reproducibility evaluation shows that the key findings of the paper have been successfully reproduced, with minor discrepancies from the original figures explained by inherent randomness.
items.abstract_zh: 论文[1]介绍了DProvDB，一种细粒度的隐私溯源框架。我们的再现性评估表明，论文的主要发现已成功再现，与原始数据存在细微差异，这是由固有的随机性造成的。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'On The Reasonable Effectiveness of Relational Diagrams'.
items.abstract: The paper [1] introduces Relational Diagrams, a visual representation of relational queries, and presents two experimental studies: a textbook analysis and a user study. The authors made all necessary files and scripts available for reproducibility through an Open Science Framework project. During the reproducibility process, a minor error was identified in the analysis of the textbook queries, though it does not affect the main conclusions of the paper. The figures reproduced for the user study are nearly identical to the ones presented in the paper. Overall, all major findings of the paper were successfully reproduced.
items.abstract_zh: 论文 [1] 介绍了关系图（关系查询的可视化表示），并提出了两项​​实验研究：教科书分析和用户研究。作者通过开放科学框架项目提供了所有必要的文件和脚本，以实现可重复性。在重现性过程中，在对教科书查询的分析中发现了一个小错误，但这并不影响论文的主要结论。用户研究中复制的数据与论文中提供的数据几乎相同。总的来说，论文的所有主要发现都被成功重现。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Proving Query Equivalence Using Linear Integer Arithmetic'.
items.abstract: The reproducibility evaluation successfully replicated key functionalities of SQLSolver. During the review process, we found a discrepancy in the verification speed regarding SQLSolver , i.e., SQLSolver 's reproduced verification latency is much higher than the result in the paper. Because after the paper was published, the authors of the paper further added some optimizations to SQLSolver , which aim at enhancing the verification capabilities and can help prove 4 more cases. However, these optimizations affect the speed of verification. After the authors of the paper provided the original version, we can successfully reproduce the main results.
items.abstract_zh: 再现性评估成功复制了 SQLSolver 的关键功能。在审核过程中，我们发现 SQLSolver 的验证速度存在差异，即 SQLSolver 的重现验证延迟远高于论文中的结果。因为论文发表后，论文作者进一步对 SQLSolver 进行了一些优化，旨在增强验证能力，可以帮助证明另外 4 个案例。然而，这些优化会影响验证速度。在论文作者提供原始版本后，我们可以成功重现主要结果。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Proximity Queries on Point Clouds using Rapid Construction Path Oracle'.
items.abstract: The reproducibility evaluation successfully replicated key functionalities of Rapid Construction path Oracle, i.e., RC-Oracle. During the review process, apart from the missing gnuplot installation instruction that was initially pointed out by the reviewer (and updated by the author), the instructions were straightforward, and the process was smooth. The authors provided datasets, codes, experiment scripts, plotting scripts and a README file that allowed the reproduction of the main results.
items.abstract_zh: 再现性评估成功复制了快速构建路径 Oracle（即 RC-Oracle）的关键功能。在审稿过程中，除了审稿人最初指出（并由作者更新）缺少gnuplot安装说明外，说明很简单，过程也很顺利。作者提供了数据集、代码、实验脚本、绘图脚本和自述文件，可以重现主要结果。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Query Refinement for Diverse Top-k Selection'.
items.abstract: The experimental evaluation of the paper has been successfully reproduced. The reproduced plots are similar to those in the paper, supporting the claims made by the authors. The provided scripts and instructions simplify the reproducibility process, but running all experiments takes multiple days.
items.abstract_zh: 论文的实验评估已成功复现。复制的情节与论文中的情节相似，支持作者的主张。提供的脚本和说明简化了重现过程，但运行所有实验需要几天时间。

items.title: Beyond Bloom: A Tutorial on Future Feature-Rich Filters.
items.abstract:
Filters, such as Bloom, quotient, and cuckoo, save space by maintaining an approximate representation of a set and occasionally returning false positives. Filters play a critical role in building modern dataintensive applications and are used across various domains such as databases, storage engines, computational biology, cyber- security, and networks. There has been extensive research on filters in the past few decades resulting in filters with much improved performance and features. Yet modern data-intensive applications are still designed around the limitations of traditional filters resulting in complex designs and sub-optimal performance.

This tutorial aims to bring together researchers at the forefront of filter data structure research to help the database community learn about the recent advancements in the theory and practice of filters. The tutorial will cover real-world case studies of redesigning applications using the modern filter APIs to achieve simplicity and improved application performance. The tutorial will further help uncover the open research problems, both in theory and systems, and increase interaction among researchers to tackle those problems.
items.abstract_zh:
过滤器（例如 Bloom、quotient 和 cuckoo）通过维护集合的近似表示并偶尔返回误报来节省空间。过滤器在构建现代数据密集型应用程序中发挥着关键作用，并广泛应用于数据库、存储引擎、计算生物学、网络安全和网络等各个领域。在过去的几十年里，人们对滤波器进行了广泛的研究，从而使滤波器的性能和功能有了很大的改进。然而，现代数据密集型应用程序仍然围绕传统过滤器的局限性进行设计，导致设计复杂且性能不佳。

本教程旨在汇集过滤器数据结构研究前沿的研究人员，帮助数据库社区了解过滤器理论和实践的最新进展。本教程将涵盖使用现代过滤器 API 重新设计应用程序的实际案例研究，以实现简单性并提高应用程序性能。该教程将进一步帮助揭示理论和系统上的开放研究问题，并增加研究人员之间的互动来解决这些问题。

items.title: Property Graph Stream Processing In Action with Seraph.
items.abstract: Given the growing popularity of graphs, data models like Property Graph and corresponding query languages like Cypher, have become popular in industry and research. However, real-time graph data analysis and management are increasingly important for today's businesses. Still, graph query languages lack the features to handle streaming graph data and their continuous query evaluation. % This demonstration showcases Seraph, a Cypher-based language supporting native continuous querying features. Seraph is formally defined, declarative, and backwards compatible with Cypher. This demonstration gives a comprehensive overview of the language with three use cases and a carefully design Web UI, allowing the execution of continuous queries on the property graphs stream.
items.abstract_zh: 鉴于图的日益普及，Property Graph 等数据模型和 Cypher 等相应的查询语言已在工业和研究领域变得流行。然而，实时图形数据分析和管理对于当今的企业来说越来越重要。尽管如此，图查询语言仍然缺乏处理流图数据及其连续查询评估的功能。 % 该演示展示了 Seraph，一种基于 Cypher 的语言，支持本机连续查询功能。 Seraph 是正式定义的、声明性的，并且与 Cypher 向后兼容。该演示通过三个用例和精心设计的 Web UI 全面概述了该语言，允许在属性图流上执行连续查询。

items.title: Similarity Joins of Sparse Features.
items.abstract: Identifying all pairs of records from two datasets whose similarity exceeds a given threshold is crucial for data cleaning and clustering. Our work on similarity-joins is motivated by detecting fraud and abuse. We focus on similarity-joins of sparse features, where records represent sparse sets, multisets, or vectors. Most state-of-the-art techniques are distributed versions of sequential algorithms. This is the reason they fail to scale when the alphabet is large, the records are large, or some elements are shared by numerous records. In this paper, we propose FastScalableSparseJoiner (FSSJ) that introduces quasi-prefix filtering, a novel flavor of prefix filtering [7] that exploits the skew in element popularity to avoid processing the most-popular elements without broadcasting the sorted elements to all executors. FastScalableSparseJoiner effectively prunes candidate pairs, and efficiently distributes the computation of partial results for load-balancing across executors. FSSJ can be adopted in any shared-nothing architecture. Based on our evaluation on synthetic and real datasets using a Spark-based implementation, FSSJ is very competitive on small datasets, and is the only algorithm that can join industry-scale datasets even with limited resources.
items.abstract_zh: 识别两个数据集中相似度超过给定阈值的所有记录对对于数据清理和聚类至关重要。我们对相似​​连接的工作是通过检测欺诈和滥用来推动的。我们关注稀疏特征的相似连接，其中记录表示稀疏集、多重集或向量。大多数最先进的技术都是顺序算法的分布式版本。这就是当字母表很大、记录很大或某些元素被大量记录共享时它们无法扩展的原因。在本文中，我们提出了 FastScalableSparseJoiner (FSSJ)，它引入了准前缀过滤，这是一种新颖的前缀过滤 [7]，它利用元素流行度的偏差来避免处理最流行的元素，而不将排序的元素广播到所有执行器。 FastScalableSparseJoiner 有效地修剪候选对，并有效地分配部分结果的计算，以在执行器之间实现负载平衡。 FSSJ 可以在任何无共享架构中采用。根据我们使用基于 Spark 的实现对合成数据集和真实数据集的评估，FSSJ 在小型数据集上非常有竞争力，并且是唯一可以在资源有限的情况下加入行业规模数据集的算法。

items.title: Demonstration of Udon: Line-by-line Debugging of User-Defined Functions in Data Workflows.
items.abstract: Many big data systems are written in languages such as C, C++, Java, and Scala for high efficiency, whereas data analysts often use Python to conduct data wrangling, statistical analysis, and machine learning. User-defined functions (UDFs) are commonly used in these systems to bridge the gap between the two ecosystems. Debugging complex UDFs in data-processing systems is challenging due to the required coordination between language debuggers and the data-processing engine, as well as the debugging overhead on large volumes of data. In this paper, we showcase Udon, a novel debugger to support line-by-line debugging of UDFs in data-processing systems. Udon encapsulates modern line-by-line debugging primitives, such as those to set breakpoints, perform code inspections, and make code modifications while executing a UDF on a single tuple. In this demonstration, we use real-world scenarios to showcase the experience of using Udon for line-by-line debugging of a UDF.
items.abstract_zh: 为了提高效率，许多大数据系统都是用 C、C++、Java 和 Scala 等语言编写的，而数据分析师经常使用 Python 来进行数据整理、统计分析和机器学习。这些系统中通常使用用户定义函数（UDF）来弥合两个生态系统之间的差距。由于语言调试器和数据处理引擎之间需要协调，以及大量数据的调试开销，在数据处理系统中调试复杂的 UDF 具有挑战性。在本文中，我们展示了 Udon，这是一种新型调试器，支持数据处理系统中 UDF 的逐行调试。 Udon 封装了现代的逐行调试原语，例如在单个元组上执行 UDF 时设置断点、执行代码检查和进行代码修改的原语。在此演示中，我们使用真实场景来展示使用 Udon 逐行调试 UDF 的体验。

items.title: ByteCard: Enhancing ByteDance's Data Warehouse with Learned Cardinality Estimation.
items.abstract:
Cardinality estimation is a critical component and a longstanding challenge in modern data warehouses. ByteHouse, ByteDance's cloud-native engine for extensive data analysis in exabyte-scale environments, serves numerous internal decision-making business scenarios. With the increasing demand for ByteHouse, cardinality estimation becomes the bottleneck for efficiently processing queries. Specifically, the existing query optimizer of ByteHouse uses the traditional Selinger-like cardinality estimator, which can produce substantial estimation errors, resulting in suboptimal query plans.

To improve cardinality estimation accuracy while maintaining a practical inference overhead, we develop a framework ByteCard that enables efficient training and integration of learned cardinality estimators. Furthermore, ByteCard adapts recent advances in cardinality estimation to build models that can balance accuracy and practicality (e.g., inference latency, model size, training overhead). We observe significant query processing speed-up in ByteHouse after replacing the existing cardinality estimator with ByteCard for several optimization scenarios. Evaluations on real-world datasets show the integration of ByteCard leads to an improvement of up to 30% in the 99th quantile of latency. At last, we share our valuable experience in engineering advanced cardinality estimators. This experience can help ByteHouse integrate more learning-based solutions on the critical query execution path in the future.
items.abstract_zh:
基数估计是现代数据仓库中的一个关键组成部分和一个长期的挑战。 ByteHouse是字节跳动的云原生引擎，用于在EB级环境中进行广泛的数据分析，服务于众多内部决策业务场景。随着 ByteHouse 需求的不断增加，基数估计成为高效处理查询的瓶颈。具体来说，ByteHouse现有的查询优化器使用传统的类似Selinger的基数估计器，这可能会产生大量的估计错误，从而导致查询计划不理想。

为了提高基数估计的准确性，同时保持实际的推理开销，我们开发了一个框架 ByteCard，该框架能够有效地训练和集成学习的基数估计器。此外，ByteCard 采用基数估计方面的最新进展来构建可以平衡准确性和实用性（例如推理延迟、模型大小、训练开销）的模型。在使用 ByteCard 替换现有的基数估计器后，我们观察到 ByteHouse 中的查询处理速度显着提高，以适应多种优化场景。对现实世界数据集的评估表明，ByteCard 的集成可将延迟的第 99 个分位数提高高达 30%。最后，我们分享了我们在设计高级基数估计器方面的宝贵经验。这种经验可以帮助ByteHouse未来在关键查询执行路径上集成更多基于学习的解决方案。

items.title: Flux: Decoupled Auto-Scaling for Heterogeneous Query Workload in Alibaba AnalyticDB.
items.abstract: Modern cloud data warehouses are integral to processing heterogeneous query workloads, which range from quick online transactions to intensive ad-hoc queries and extract, transform, load (ETL) processes. The synchronization of heterogeneous workloads, particularly the blend of short and long-running queries, often degrades performance due to intricate concurrency controls and cooperative multi-tasking execution models. Additionally, the auto-scaling mechanisms for mixed workloads can lead to spikes in demand and underutilized resources, impacting both performance and cost-efficiency. This paper introduces the Flux, a cloud-native workload auto-scaling platform designed for Alibaba AnalyticDB, which implements a pioneering decoupled auto-scaling architecture. By separating the scaling mechanisms for short and long-running queries, Flux not only resolves performance bottlenecks but also harnesses the elasticity of serverless container instances for on-demand resource provisioning. Our extensive evaluations demonstrate Flux's superiority over traditional scaling methods, with up to a 75% reduction in query response time (RT), a 19.0% increase in resource utilization ratio, and a 77.8% decrease in cost overhead.
items.abstract_zh: 现代云数据仓库是处理异构查询工作负载不可或缺的一部分，这些工作负载的范围从快速在线事务到密集的临时查询以及提取、转换、加载 (ETL) 流程。由于复杂的并发控制和协作多任务执行模型，异构工作负载的同步，特别是短时间和长时间运行的查询的混合，通常会降低性能。此外，混合工作负载的自动扩展机制可能会导致需求激增和资源利用不足，从而影响性能和成本效率。本文介绍了Flux，这是一个为阿里AnalyticDB设计的云原生工作负载自动伸缩平台，它实现了首创的解耦自动伸缩架构。通过分离短查询和长时间查询的扩展机制，Flux 不仅解决了性能瓶颈，还利用无服务器容器实例的弹性来按需配置资源。我们的广泛评估证明了 Flux 相对于传统扩展方法的优越性，查询响应时间 (RT) 降低了 75%，资源利用率提高了 19.0%，成本开销降低了 77.8%。

items.title: Extending Polaris to Support Transactions.
items.abstract: In Polaris [1], we introduced a cloud-native distributed query processor to perform analytics at scale. In this paper, we extend the underlying Polaris distributed computation framework, which can be thought of as a read-only transaction engine, to execute general transactions (including updates, deletes, inserts and bulk loads, in addition to queries) for Tier 1 warehousing workloads in a highly performant and predictable manner. We take advantage of the immutability of data files in log-structured data stores and build on SQL Server transaction management to deliver full transactional support with Snapshot Isolation semantics, including multi-table and multi-statement transactions. With the enhancements described in this paper, Polaris supports both query processing and transactions for T-SQL in Microsoft Fabric [2].
items.abstract_zh: 在 Polaris [1] 中，我们引入了云原生分布式查询处理器来大规模执行分析。在本文中，我们扩展了底层的 Polaris 分布式计算框架（可以将其视为只读事务引擎），以高性能和可预测的方式为第 1 层仓储工作负载执行一般事务（除了查询之外，还包括更新、删除、插入和批量加载）。我们利用日志结构数据存储中数据文件的不变性，并构建 SQL Server 事务管理，通过快照隔离语义提供完整的事务支持，包括多表和多语句事务。通过本文中描述的增强功能，Polaris 支持 Microsoft Fabric [2] 中的 T-SQL 查询处理和事务。

items.title: Distributed Transaction Processing in Untrusted Environments.
items.abstract: Byzantine Fault-Tolerant (BFT) protocols have recently been extensively used by distributed and decentralized data management systems with non-trustworthy infrastructures to establish consensus on the order of transactions. BFT protocols cover a broad spectrum of design dimensions from infrastructure settings, such as the communication topology, to more technical features, such as commitment strategy and even fundamental social choice properties like order-fairness. The proliferation of different protocols has made it difficult to navigate the BFT landscape, let alone determine the protocol that best meets application needs. In this tutorial, we discuss BFT protocols that are used in modern large-scale data management systems, present a design space consisting of a set of design dimensions and explore several design choices that capture the trade-offs between different design space dimensions. The presented design space and its design choices will help developers analyze BFT protocols, understand how different protocols are related to each other, and find the protocol that best fits their needs.
items.abstract_zh: 拜占庭容错（BFT）协议最近被具有不可信基础设施的分布式和去中心化数据管理系统广泛使用，以建立对交易顺序的共识。 BFT 协议涵盖了广泛的设计维度，从基础设施设置（例如通信拓扑）到更多技术功能（例如承诺策略）甚至基本的社会选择属性（例如订单公平）。不同协议的激增使得浏览 BFT 领域变得困难，更不用说确定最能满足应用程序需求的协议了。在本教程中，我们讨论现代大规模数据管理系统中使用的 BFT 协议，提出由一组设计维度组成的设计空间，并探索几种设计选择，以捕获不同设计空间维度之间的权衡。所呈现的设计空间及其设计选择将帮助开发人员分析 BFT 协议，了解不同协议之间的相互关系，并找到最适合他们需求的协议。

items.title: First Workshop on Governance, Understanding and Integration of Data for Effective and Responsible AI (GUIDE-AI).
items.abstract: With the recent advancements in artificial intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML), data-driven automated systems are being deployed in numerous high-stakes applications. Central to AI's effectiveness is its foundation in data. This workshop aims to bring together researchers from academia and industry to discuss the role of data management to guide the trustworthy design of AI-based applications. We plan the first edition of the workshop to include invited talks and a panel discussion with researchers from neighboring communities like ML, FAccT, HCI and Theoretical Computer science. The workshop aims to create a collaborative platform for these diverse communities to contribute to the evolving narrative of responsible AI development.
items.abstract_zh: 随着人工智能 (AI) 和机器学习 (ML) 的最新进展，数据驱动的自动化系统正在众多高风险应用中部署。人工智能有效性的核心是其数据基础。本次研讨会旨在聚集学术界和工业界的研究人员，讨论数据管理在指导基于人工智能的应用程序的可信设计方面的作用。我们计划第一届研讨会包括受邀演讲以及与来自邻近社区（例如 ML、FAccT、HCI 和理论计算机科学）的研究人员进行的小组讨论。该研讨会旨在为这些不同的社区创建一个协作平台，为负责任的人工智能开发的不断发展做出贡献。

items.title: The Limitations of Data, Machine Learning and Us.
items.abstract: Machine learning (ML), particularly deep learning, is being used everywhere. However, not always is applied well or has ethical and/or scientific issues. In this keynote we first do a deep dive in the limitations of supervised ML and data, its key input. We cover small data, datification, bias, and evaluating success instead of harm, among other limitations. The second part is about ourselves using ML, including different types of social limitations and human incompetence such as cognitive biases, pseudoscience, or unethical applications. These limitations have harmful consequences such as discrimination, misinformation, and mental health issues, to mention just a few. In the final part we discuss regulation on the use of AI and responsible principles that can mitigate the problems outlined above.
items.abstract_zh: 机器学习（ML），特别是深度学习，正在被广泛使用。然而，并不总是得到很好的应用，或者存在伦理和/或科学问题。在本次主题演讲中，我们首先深入探讨监督机器学习及其关键输入数据的局限性。我们涵盖小数据、数据化、偏见、评估成功而不是伤害等限制。第二部分是关于我们自己如何使用机器学习，包括不同类型的社会限制和人类无能，例如认知偏见、伪科学或不道德的应用。这些限制会产生有害后果，例如歧视、错误信息和心理健康问题等等。在最后一部分中，我们讨论了人工智能使用的监管以及可以缓解上述问题的负责任原则。

items.title: Rock: Cleaning Data by Embedding ML in Logic Rules.
items.abstract: We introduce Rock, a system for cleaning relational data. Rock implements a framework that unifies machine learning (ML) and logic deduction by embedding ML classifiers in rules as predicates. In a unified process, it identifies tuples that refer to the same real-world entity, catches semantic inconsistencies among the entities, deduces the timeliness of the attribute values of the entities, and imputes missing values by possibly extracting data from knowledge graphs. That is, Rock conducts entity resolution, conflict resolution, incomplete information imputation and timeliness deduction in the same process, makes use of their interactions and improves the overall quality of the data. Moreover, Rock supports methods, batch and incremental, for discovering rules from real-life data, detecting errors with the learned rules, accumulating ground truth, and fixing the errors, such that the corrections are logical consequences of the rules and ground truth. We present the design and implementation of Rock. We evaluate the scalability and accuracy of Rock, and share lessons learned from a variety of real-life applications.
items.abstract_zh: 我们介绍Rock，一个用于清理关系数据的系统。 Rock 通过将 ML 分类器作为谓词嵌入规则中，实现了一个统一机器学习 (ML) 和逻辑演绎的框架。在统一的过程中，它识别引用同一现实世界实体的元组，捕获实体之间的语义不一致，推断实体属性值的时效性，并通过可能从知识图谱中提取数据来估算缺失值。即Rock在同一过程中进行实体解析、冲突解析、不完全信息插补和时效性推导，利用它们的相互作用，提高数据的整体质量。此外，Rock 支持批量和增量方法，用于从现实数据中发现规则，用学习的规则检测错误，积累基本事实，并修复错误，以便更正是规则和基本事实的逻辑结果。我们介绍 Rock 的设计和实现。我们评估 Rock 的可扩展性和准确性，并分享从各种实际应用程序中吸取的经验教训。

items.title: OpenIVM: a SQL-to-SQL Compiler for Incremental Computations.
items.abstract:
This demonstration presents a new Open Source SQL-to-SQL compiler for Incremental View Maintenance (IVM). While previous systems, such as DBToaster, implemented computational functionality for IVM in a separate system, the core principle of OpenIVM is to make use of existing SQL query processing engines and perform all IVM computations via SQL. This approach enables the integration of IVM in these systems without code duplication. Also, it eases its use in cross-system IVM, i.e. to orchestrate an HTAP system in which one (OLTP) DBMS provides insertions/updates/deletes (deltas), which are propagated using SQL into another (OLAP) DBMS, hosting materialized views. Our system compiles view definitions into SQL to eventually propagate deltas into the table that materializes the view, following the principles of DBSP.

Under the hood, OpenIVM uses the DuckDB library to compile (parse, transform, optimize) the materialized view maintenance logic. We demonstrate OpenIVM in action (i) as the core of a DuckDB extension module that adds IVM functionality to it and (ii) powering cross-system IVM for HTAP, with PostgreSQL handling updates on base tables and DuckDB hosting materialized views on these.
items.abstract_zh:
该演示展示了一个用于增量视图维护 (IVM) 的新开源 SQL-to-SQL 编译器。虽然以前的系统（例如 DBToaster）在单独的系统中实现了 IVM 的计算功能，但 OpenIVM 的核心原理是利用现有的 SQL 查询处理引擎并通过 SQL 执行所有 IVM 计算。这种方法可以将 IVM 集成到这些系统中，而无需重复代码。此外，它还简化了其在跨系统 IVM 中的使用，即编排 HTAP 系统，其中一个 (OLTP) DBMS 提供插入/更新/删除（增量），这些插入/更新/删除（增量）使用 SQL 传播到另一个托管物化视图的 (OLAP) DBMS。我们的系统将视图定义编译为 SQL，最终将增量传播到具体化视图的表中，遵循 DBSP 的原则。

在底层，OpenIVM 使用 DuckDB 库来编译（解析、转换、优化）物化视图维护逻辑。我们演示了 OpenIVM 的实际应用：(i) 作为 DuckDB 扩展模块的核心，向其添加 IVM 功能；(ii) 为 HTAP 提供跨系统 IVM，其中 PostgreSQL 处理基表更新，DuckDB 托管这些表的物化视图。

items.title: The Game Of Recourse: Simulating Algorithmic Recourse over Time to Improve Its Reliability and Fairness.
items.abstract: Algorithmic recourse, or providing recommendations to individuals who receive an unfavorable outcome from an algorithmic system on how they can take action and change that outcome, is an important tool for giving individuals agency against algorithmic decision systems. Unfortunately, research on algorithmic recourse faces a fundamental challenge: there are no publicly available datasets on algorithmic recourse. In this work, we begin to explore a solution to this challenge by creating an agent-based simulation called The Game of Recourse (an homage to Conway's Game of Life) to synthesize realistic algorithmic recourse data. We designed The Game of Recourse with a focus on reliability and fairness, two areas of critical importance in socio-technical systems. You can access the application at https://game-of-recourse.streamlit.app.
items.abstract_zh: 算法追索权，或者向从算法系统收到不利结果的个人提供如何采取行动并改变结果的建议，是赋予个人针对算法决策系统的代理权的重要工具。不幸的是，算法追索的研究面临着一个根本性的挑战：没有关于算法追索的公开数据集。在这项工作中，我们开始探索这一挑战的解决方案，通过创建名为“追索游戏”（向康威的生命游戏致敬）的基于代理的模拟来合成现实的算法追索数据。我们设计追索游戏的重点是可靠性和公平性，这是社会技术系统中至关重要的两个领域。您可以通过 https://game-of-recourse.streamlit.app 访问该应用程序。

items.title: Cognitive Psychology Meets Data Management: State of the Art and Future Directions.
items.abstract: Data definition and data manipulation language platforms of a DBMS are oftenhuman-centric. For instance, declarative queries are often formulated by humans or their results are directly consumed by humans. Consequently, superior and effective design of these platforms needs to be informed by theories fromcognitive psychology, a branch of psychology that studies how individuals think and process information. In this tutorial, we review and summarize the state of the art in the emerging paradigm ofpsychology theory-informed design ofhuman-centric components of a DBMS. In this paradigm, the design of the human-centric components is guided by theories from cognitive psychology in addition to theories from computer science. We review techniques and frameworks that embrace this paradigm in the arena of database querying. To guide future work in this arena, the tutorial proposes open problems and new research directions.
items.abstract_zh: DBMS 的数据定义和数据操作语言平台通常以人为中心。例如，声明性查询通常由人类制定，或者它们的结果由人类直接使用。因此，这些平台的卓越而有效的设计需要借鉴认知心理学的理论，认知心理学是研究个人如何思考和处理信息的心理学分支。在本教程中，我们回顾并总结了以心理学理论为基础的 DBMS 以人为中心的组件设计的新兴范式的最新技术。在这种范式中，以人为中心的组件的设计除了计算机科学的理论之外还受到认知心理学的理论指导。我们回顾了数据库查询领域采用这种范例的技术和框架。为了指导该领域的未来工作，本教程提出了开放问题和新的研究方向。

items.title: International Workshop on Data Management on New Hardware (DaMoN).
items.abstract: New hardware, such as multi-core CPUs, GPUs, FPGAs, new memory and storage technologies, low-power devices, bring new challenges and opportunities in optimizing database systems performance. Consequently, exploiting the characteristics of modern hardware has become an important topic of database systems research. In the last two decades, the DaMoN Workshop has established itself as the primary database venue to present ideas on how to exploit new hardware for data management, in particular how to improve performance or scalability of databases, how new hardware unlocks new database application scenarios, and how data management could benefit from future hardware.
items.abstract_zh: 新的硬件，如多核CPU、GPU、FPGA、新的内存和存储技术、低功耗设备，给优化数据库系统性能带来了新的挑战和机遇。因此，利用现代硬件的特性已成为数据库系统研究的一个重要课题。在过去的二十年中，DaMoN Workshop 已成为主要的数据库场所，提出如何利用新硬件进行数据管理的想法，特别是如何提高数据库的性能或可扩展性、新硬件如何解锁新的数据库应用场景以及数据管理如何从未来的硬件中受益。

items.title: Making Data Management Better with Vectorized Query Processing.
items.abstract: Vectorized query processing is a query processing technique that 20 years ago was introduced in the Vectorwise database system, originally developed in the CWI database architectures research group. Nowadays, it is employed by most analytical data systems, from recent systems like DuckDB, Clickhouse, Velox, DataFusion and Polars to analytical cloud services such as Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks and MotherDuck. The keynote will tell the story of vectorized query processing, as it evolved in research and industry over the past two decades. It will conclude with a reflection on the purpose of our research ("making data management better").
items.abstract_zh: 矢量化查询处理是 20 年前在 Vectorwise 数据库系统中引入的查询处理技术，最初是由 CWI 数据库架构研究小组开发的。如今，大多数分析数据系统都采用它，从 DuckDB、Clickhouse、Velox、DataFusion 和 Polars 等最新系统到 Snowflake、BigQuery、Databricks 和 MotherDuck 等分析云服务。主题演讲将讲述矢量化查询处理在过去二十年在研究和行业中的发展历程。最后将反思我们的研究目的（“让数据管理变得更好”）。

items.title: An Overview of Continuous Querying in (Modern) Data Systems.
items.abstract: Continuous queries, also known as standing or streaming queries, are a class of queries that continuously monitor data sources over time, remaining active until explicitly terminated. This concept was introduced in 1992 by Terry and colleagues to address the need to access data that changes over time. Since then, the domain of continuous queries has seen significant development, research, and application in various data systems, including multiple Database Management Systems (DBMS). Notably, the past five years have marked the rise of Streaming Databases (SDS), and DBMSs that integrate continuous queries with traditional query responses.This growth reflects both industrial and academic interest in the field. Initially, continuous queries were primarily focused on the relational model, with their semantics expressed through extended algebras, calculi, or denotational semantics. However, more recently, there has been a shift towards applying continuous queries in the context of connected data. This includes exploration and abstraction in Graph DBMS, Knowledge Graphs, and Knowledge Evolution, expanding beyond the initial focus on relational models. This tutorial delves into a comprehensive survey of Continuous Queries in past and modern data systems. In addition to the historical perspective, the tutorial will focus on the latest developments in Streaming Databases and the continuous processing of streaming graphs.
items.abstract_zh: 连续查询，也称为常设查询或流式查询，是一类随着时间的推移持续监视数据源的查询，在明确终止之前保持活动状态。这一概念由 Terry 及其同事于 1992 年提出，旨在满足访问随时间变化的数据的需求。从那时起，连续查询领域在各种数据系统（包括多个数据库管理系统（DBMS））中得到了显着的发展、研究和应用。值得注意的是，过去五年标志着流数据库 (SDS) 以及将连续查询与传统查询响应集成在一起的 DBMS 的兴起。这种增长反映了工业界和学术界对该领域的兴趣。最初，连续查询主要关注关系模型，其语义通过扩展代数、演算或指称语义来表达。然而，最近，人们开始转向在互联数据的背景下应用连续查询。这包括图 DBMS、知识图和知识演化的探索和抽象，超出了最初对关系模型的关注。本教程深入研究了过去和现代数据系统中连续查询的全面调查。除了历史视角之外，本教程还将重点关注流数据库的最新发展和流图的连续处理。

items.title: The Future of Graph Analytics.
items.abstract:
In the last two decades, we have been witnessing high demand for graph-based technologies in industry. On the research side, several recent advances have been made about large-scale graph processing, graph analytical systems and graph databases. The landscape of graph query languages is currently evolving with the definition of new standards, and the need for domain-specific languages to express graph algorithmic and analytical primitives will continue and increase in the next future.

In this SIGMOD panel, we will discuss the impact of the above changes on the future of graph analytics. Is there a demand for more expressive languages and libraries for analyzing relationships in a graph? Are new hybrid OLTP/OLAP architectures required with improved performance and scalability? What are the graph analytical workloads and benchmarks that users expect on real-world graph applications? What will be the impact of graph ML on graph analytical systems? How to adapt these systems to the dynamic changes that are ubiquitous for graph-shaped data? These and other questions will be addressed in the panel.
items.abstract_zh:
在过去的二十年中，我们目睹了工业界对基于图的技术的高需求。在研究方面，大规模图处理、图分析系统和图数据库方面最近取得了一些进展。目前，图查询语言的格局正在随着新标准的定义而不断发展，并且对特定于领域的语言来表达图算法和分析原语的需求将在未来继续并增加。

在本次 SIGMOD 小组中，我们将讨论上述变化对图分析未来的影响。是否需要更具表现力的语言和库来分析图表中的关系？是否需要新的混合 OLTP/OLAP 架构来提高性能和可扩展性？用户在现实世界的图形应用程序中期望的图形分析工作负载和基准是什么？图机器学习对图分析系统有何影响？如何使这些系统适应图形数据普遍存在的动态变化？这些问题和其他问题将在小组讨论中得到解决。

items.title: Seventh International Workshop on Exploiting Artificial Intelligence Techniques for Data Management (aiDM).
items.abstract: Recent advances in AI techniques, as well as enabling hardware and infrastructure, have led to the integration of AI across wide-ranging domains and tasks. In particular, AI has been used to handle various types of data (including numerical, textual and image data) and has been adopted in large-scale distributed systems. From a data management perspective, this calls for the harnessing of state-of-the-art AI solutions for data management tasks and systems. aiDM is a full-day workshop that offers a stage for innovative interdisciplinary research that studies the interaction between AI and data management and develops new AI technologies for data-related tasks. This year, aiDM'24 particularly focuses on the transparent exploitation of AI techniques (e.g., using Generative AI frameworks) for data management for enterprise class workloads.
items.abstract_zh: 人工智能技术以及支持硬件和基础设施的最新进展导致人工智能在广泛的领域和任务中的集成。特别是，人工智能已被用于处理各种类型的数据（包括数字、文本和图像数据），并已在大规模分布式系统中得到采用。从数据管理的角度来看，这需要利用最先进的人工智能解决方案来执行数据管理任务和系统。 aiDM 是一个全天的研讨会，为创新的跨学科研究提供了一个舞台，研究人工智能和数据管理之间的相互作用，并为数据相关任务开发新的人工智能技术。今年，aiDM'24 特别关注人工智能技术的透明利用（例如，使用生成式人工智能框架）进行企业级工作负载的数据管理。

items.title: Demonstrating REmatch: A Novel RegEx Engine for Finding all Matches.
items.abstract: In this demonstration we showcase REmatch, a regular expression (RegEx) engine built to find all matches of a given pattern in a document. REmatch is based on the theory of enumeration algorithms, and it extends the capability of classical regex engines with the ability to find nested and overlapping matches with a simple and intuitive syntax, and no need to use non-standard operators, while at the same time maintaining efficient performance. The algorithmic core of REmatch is an algorithm that builds a compressed representation of all matching results and enumerates them on demand in time proportional to writing them down symbol by symbol. For this demonstration, we developed a simple Web interface to access the REmatch engine, available at https://rematch.cl, and will showcase the utility of the engine in the context of analysing DNA sequences, linguistic analysis, among others. For this, we have prepared a series of examples that illustrate the expressiveness and ease of use of REmatch. Additionally, at the demo we will have a series of challenge tasks, asking attendees to capture the result set of a simple REmatch expression using their favourite RegEx engine.
items.abstract_zh: 在此演示中，我们展示了 REmatch，这是一个正则表达式 (RegEx) 引擎，旨在查找文档中给定模式的所有匹配项。 REmatch基于枚举算法的理论，它扩展了经典正则表达式引擎的能力，能够以简单直观的语法查找嵌套和重叠匹配，并且无需使用非标准运算符，同时保持高效的性能。 REmatch 的算法核心是一种算法，该算法构建所有匹配结果的压缩表示，并按照与逐个符号写下它们成比例的时间按需枚举它们。对于本次演示，我们开发了一个简单的 Web 界面来访问 REmatch 引擎（可在 https://rematch.cl 上获取），并将展示该引擎在分析 DNA 序列、语言分析等方面的实用性。为此，我们准备了一系列示例来说明 REmatch 的表现力和易用性。此外，在演示中，我们将进行一系列挑战任务，要求与会者使用他们最喜欢的 RegEx 引擎捕获简单 REmatch 表达式的结果集。

items.title: Unified Query Optimization in the Fabric Data Warehouse.
items.abstract: Over a decade ago, Microsoft introduced Parallel Data Warehouse (PDW), a massively parallel processing system to manage and query large amounts of data. Its optimizer was built by reusing SQL Server's infrastructure with minimal changes, which was an effective approach to bring cost-based query optimization quickly to PDW. Over time, learnings from production as well as architectural changes in the product (such as moving from an appliance form factor to the cloud, separation of compute and storage, and serverless components) required evolving the query optimizer in Fabric DW, the latest offering from Microsoft in the cloud data warehouse space. In this paper we describe the changes to the optimization process in Fabric DW, compare them to the earlier architecture used in PDW, and validate our new approach.
items.abstract_zh: 十多年前，微软推出了并行数据仓库（PDW），这是一个用于管理和查询大量数据的大规模并行处理系统。其优化器是通过重用 SQL Server 的基础架构而构建的，只需进行最少的更改，这是将基于成本的查询优化快速引入 PDW 的有效方法。随着时间的推移，从生产中学到的知识以及产品的架构变化（例如从设备外形尺寸转向云、计算和存储分离以及无服务器组件）需要改进 Fabric DW 中的查询优化器，Fabric DW 是 Microsoft 在云数据仓库领域的最新产品。在本文中，我们描述了 Fabric DW 中优化过程的变化，将它们与 PDW 中使用的早期架构进行比较，并验证我们的新方法。

items.title: Automated Clustering Recommendation With Database Zone Maps.
items.abstract: Zone maps are a data access structure provided in multiple data warehousing products [1, 3, 5, 14, 20], that reduce the cost of data access for a query by advance pruning of irrelevant data blocks. This is achieved by dividing the table into zones and storing for each zone, the minimum and maximum value of selected columns. The structures used to store these values are called zone maps. Zone maps are most effective for a query if the data is sorted (or clustered) on a column that the query has filters on, and if it also has zone maps constructed on the column. More selective filters are likely to lead to better performance. But as filter predicates vary from query to query, it is not straightforward to identify a single clustering that is effective across the entire workload. This paper describes a novel method for automatically analyzing a workload and recommending a clustering solution, as well as the zone maps to construct for improved performance. It works for both linear and interleaved (z-order) clustering, the two most commonly used clustering methodologies. In our experiments on the TPCH bench- mark and customer datasets, this approach provides significant performance improvements over the baseline. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work to address the automated cluster- ing (auto-clustering) problem. The method described here has been implemented and will be released in an upcoming version of the Oracle Autonomous Database.
items.abstract_zh: 区域映射是多种数据仓库产品[1,3,5,14,20]中提供的一种数据访问结构，它通过提前修剪不相关的数据块来降低查询的数据访问成本。这是通过将表划分为区域并为每个区域存储所选列的最小值和最大值来实现的。用于存储这些值的结构称为区域映射。如果数据在查询具有筛选器的列上排序（或聚集），并且还具有在该列上构建的区域映射，则区域映射对于查询最有效。更具选择性的过滤器可能会带来更好的性能。但由于过滤谓词因查询而异，因此识别在整个工作负载中有效的单个集群并不简单。本文描述了一种自动分析工作负载并推荐集群解决方案的新颖方法，以及为提高性能而构建的区域映射。它适用于线性和交错（z 顺序）聚类，这两种最常用的聚类方法。在我们对 TPCH 基准和客户数据集进行的实验中，这种方法比基线提供了显着的性能改进。据我们所知，这是第一个解决自动聚类（auto-clustering）问题的工作。这里描述的方法已经实现，并将在即将发布的 Oracle 自治数据库版本中发布。

items.title: PG-Triggers: Triggers for Property Graphs.
items.abstract:
Graph databases are emerging as the leading data management technology for storing large knowledge graphs; significant efforts are ongoing to produce new standards (such as the Graph Query Language, GQL), as well as enrich them with properties, types, schemas, and keys. In this article, we introduce PG-Triggers, a complete proposal for adding triggers to Property Graphs, along the direction marked by the SQL3 Standard.

We define the syntax and semantics of PG-Triggers and then illustrate how they can be implemented on top of Neo4j, one of the most popular graph databases. In particular, we introduce a syntax-directed translation from PG-Triggers into Neo4j, which makes use of the so-called APOC triggers; APOC is a community-contributed library for augmenting the Cypher query language supported by Neo4j. We also cover Memgraph, and show that our approach applies to this system in a similar way. We illustrate the use of PG-Triggers through a life science application inspired by the COVID-19 pandemic.

The main objective of this article is to introduce an active database standard for graph databases as a first-class citizen at a time when reactive graph management is in its infancy, so as to minimize the conversion efforts towards a full-fledged standard proposal.
items.abstract_zh:
图数据库正在成为存储大型知识图的领先数据管理技术；我们正在付出巨大的努力来制定新标准（例如图形查询语言，GQL），并通过属性、类型、模式和键来丰富它们。在本文中，我们沿着 SQL3 标准标记的方向，介绍 PG-Triggers，这是一个向属性图添加触发器的完整提案。

我们定义 PG-Triggers 的语法和语义，然后说明如何在最流行的图形数据库之一 Neo4j 之上实现它们。特别是，我们引入了从 PG-Triggers 到 Neo4j 的语法定向转换，它利用了所谓的 APOC 触发器； APOC 是一个社区贡献的库，用于增强 Neo4j 支持的 Cypher 查询语言。我们还介绍了 Memgraph，并表明我们的方法以类似的方式适用于该系统。我们通过受 COVID-19 大流行启发的生命科学应用来说明 PG-Triggers 的使用。

本文的主要目标是在反应式图管理还处于起步阶段时，为图数据库引入一个主动数据库标准，作为一等公民，从而最大限度地减少向成熟标准提案的转换工作。

items.title: PLUTUS: Understanding Data Distribution Tailoring for Machine Learning.
items.abstract: Existing data debugging tools allow users to trace model performance problems all the way to the data by efficiently identifying slices (conjunctions of features and values) for which a trained model performs significantly worse than the entire dataset. To ensure accurate and fair models, one solution is to acquire enough data for these slices. In addition to crowdsourcing, recent data acquisition techniques design cost-effective algorithms to obtain such data from a union of external sources such as data lakes and data markets. We demonstrate PLUTUS, a tool for human-in-the-loop and model-aware data acquisition pipeline, on SystemDS, as an open source ML system for the end-to-end data science lifecycle. In PLUTUS, a user can efficiently identify problematic slices, connect to external data sources, and acquire the right amount of data for these slices in a cost-effective manner.
items.abstract_zh: 现有的数据调试工具允许用户通过有效地识别训练模型的性能明显低于整个数据集的切片（特征和值的结合）来跟踪模型性能问题一直到数据。为了确保模型的准确性和公平性，一种解决方案是为这些切片获取足够的数据。除了众包之外，最近的数据获取技术还设计了具有成本效益的算法，以从数据湖和数据市场等外部来源的联合中获取此类数据。我们在 SystemDS 上演示了 PLUTUS，这是一种用于人机交互和模型感知数据采集管道的工具，作为端到端数据科学生命周期的开源机器学习系统。在 PLUTUS 中，用户可以有效地识别有问题的切片，连接到外部数据源，并以经济高效的方式获取这些切片的适量数据。

items.title: Data-Juicer: A One-Stop Data Processing System for Large Language Models.
items.abstract:
The immense evolution in Large Language Models (LLMs) has underscored the importance of massive, heterogeneous, and high-quality data. A data recipe is a mixture of data from different sources for training LLMs, which plays a vital role in LLMs' performance. Existing open-source tools for LLM data processing are mostly tailored for specific data recipes. To continuously uncover the potential of LLMs, incorporate data from new sources, and improve LLMs' performance, we build a new system named Data-Juicer, with which we can efficiently generate diverse data recipes, explore different possibilities in forming data mixtures, and evaluate their effects on model performance. Different from traditional data-analytics pipelines, Data-Juicer faces some unique challenges. Firstly, the possible data sources for forming data recipes are truly heterogeneous and massive with various qualities. Secondly, it is extremely expensive to precisely evaluate data recipes' impact on LLMs' performance. Thirdly, the end users of Data-Juicer, model developers, need sufficient flexibility to configure and evaluate different data recipes.

Data-Juicer features a fine-grained abstraction of pipelines for constructing data recipes, with over 50 built-in operators for easy composition and extension. By incorporating visualization and auto-evaluation capabilities, Data-Juicer enables a timely feedback loop for both LLM pre-training and fine-tuning. Further, Data-Juicer is optimized and integrated with ecosystems for LLM training, evaluation, and distributed computing. The data recipes derived with Data-Juicer gain notable improvements on state-of-the-art LLMs, by up to 7.45% increase in averaged score across 16 benchmarks and 17.5% higher win rate in pair-wise GPT-4 evaluations. Our system, recipes, and tutorials are released, calling for broader data-centric research on training and understanding LLMs.
items.abstract_zh:
大型语言模型 (LLM) 的巨大发展凸显了海量、异构和高质量数据的重要性。数据配方是来自不同来源的数据的混合，用于训练法学硕士，这对法学硕士的表现起着至关重要的作用。现有的 LLM 数据处理开源工具大多是针对特定数据配方量身定制的。为了不断发掘LLM的潜力，整合新来源的数据并提高LLM的性能，我们构建了一个名为Data-Juicer的新系统，通过它我们可以有效地生成不同的数据配方，探索形成数据混合的不同可能性，并评估它们对模型性能的影响。与传统的数据分析管道不同，Data-Juicer 面临着一些独特的挑战。首先，形成数据配方的可能数据源确实是异构的、海量的、质量各异的。其次，精确评估数据配方对法学硕士绩效的影响极其昂贵。第三，Data-Juicer的最终用户，即模型开发人员，需要足够的灵活性来配置和评估不同的数据配方。

Data-Juicer 具有用于构建数据配方的细粒度管道抽象，具有 50 多个内置运算符，可轻松组合和扩展。通过整合可视化和自动评估功能，Data-Juicer 为 LLM 预训练和微调提供了及时的反馈循环。此外，Data-Juicer 还针对 LLM 培训、评估和分布式计算进行了优化并与生态系统集成。使用 Data-Juicer 得出的数据配方在最先进的 LLM 上获得了显着改进，16 个基准测试的平均分数提高了 7.45%，成对 GPT-4 评估的获胜率提高了 17.5%。我们的系统、配方和教程已发布，呼吁对法学硕士的培训和理解进行更广泛的以数据为中心的研究。

items.title: Comquest: Large Scale User Comment Crawling and Integration.
items.abstract: User-generated content like comments are valuable sources for various downstream applications. However, access to user comments data is often limited to specific platforms or outlets, which imposes a great limitation on the available data, and may not provide a representative sample of opinions from a diverse population on a particular event. This paper presents a comment crawling system that leverages the Web API of popular third-party commenting systems to collect comments from a large number of websites integrated with the commenting systems. Given a target page, the crawling system utilizes a deep learning model to extract API parameters and send HTTP requests to the API to retrieve comments. The system, Comquest, that we propose to demo is news-oriented and crawls comments regarding specific news topics/stories. Comquest can work with any website that allows commenting. Comquest provides a useful tool for collecting comments that represent a wider range of opinions, stances, and sentiments from websites on a global scale.
items.abstract_zh: 用户生成的内容（例如评论）是各种下游应用程序的宝贵来源。然而，对用户评论数据的访问通常仅限于特定平台或渠道，这对可用数据造成了很大的限制，并且可能无法提供不同人群对特定事件的代表性意见样本。本文提出了一种评论抓取系统，该系统利用流行的第三方评论系统的Web API来收集大量与评论系统集成的网站的评论。给定一个目标页面，爬虫系统利用深度学习模型提取API参数并向API发送HTTP请求以检索评论。我们建议演示的系统 Comquest 是面向新闻的，会抓取有关特定新闻主题/故事的评论。 Comquest 可以与任何允许评论的网站一起使用。 Comquest 提供了一个有用的工具，用于从全球范围内的网站收集代表更广泛的意见、立场和情绪的评论。

items.title: ChatPipe: Orchestrating Data Preparation Pipelines by Optimizing Human-ChatGPT Interactions.
items.abstract: Orchestrating a high-quality data preparation program is essential for successful machine learning (ML), but it is known to be time and effort consuming. Despite the impressive capabilities of large language models like ChatGPT in generating programs by inter- acting with users through natural language prompts, there are still limitations. Specifically, a user must provide specific prompts to iteratively guide ChatGPT in improving data preparation programs, which requires a certain level of expertise in programming, the dataset used and the ML task. Moreover, once a program has been generated, it is non-trivial to revisit a previous version or make changes to the program without starting the process over again. In this paper, we present ChatPipe, a novel system designed to facilitate seamless interaction between users and ChatGPT. Chat- Pipe provides users with effective recommendation on next data preparation operations, and guides ChatGPT to generate program for the operations. Also, ChatPipe enables users to easily roll back to previous versions of the program, which facilitates more efficient experimentation and testing. We have developed a web application for ChatPipe and prepared several real-world ML tasks from Kaggle, which can demonstrate the capabilities of ChatPipe.
items.abstract_zh: 编排高质量的数据准备程序对于成功的机器学习 (ML) 至关重要，但众所周知，它非常耗时和费力。尽管像 ChatGPT 这样的大型语言模型在通过自然语言提示与用户交互来生成程序方面具有令人印象深刻的能力，但仍然存在局限性。具体来说，用户必须提供特定的提示来迭代指导 ChatGPT 改进数据准备程序，这需要在编程、使用的数据集和 ML 任务方面具有一定水平的专业知识。此外，一旦生成了程序，重新访问以前的版本或对程序进行更改而不重新开始该过程就很重要。在本文中，我们提出了 ChatPipe，这是一个旨在促进用户和 ChatGPT 之间无缝交互的新颖系统。 Chat-Pipe为用户下一步的数据准备操作提供有效的建议，并指导ChatGPT生成该操作的程序。此外，ChatPipe 使用户能够轻松回滚到程序的先前版本，从而有利于更高效的实验和测试。我们为 ChatPipe 开发了一个 Web 应用程序，并准备了几个来自 Kaggle 的实际 ML 任务，这可以演示 ChatPipe 的功能。

items.title: User-friendly, Interactive, and Configurable Explanations for Graph Neural Networks with Graph Views.
items.abstract:
Explaining the behavior of graph neural networks (GNNs) has become critical due to their "black-box'' nature, especially in the context of analytical tasks such as graph classification. Current approaches are limited to providing explanations for individual instances or specific class labels and may return large explanation structures that are hard to access, nor directly queryable. In this paper, we present GVEX [1] ( G raph V iews for GNN EX planation) -- our system developed to offer user-friendly, interactive, and configurable explanations for GNNs based on graph views.

GVEX provides a configuration component to enable users to easily select a desired number of important nodes from different classes, thereby generating explanations tailored to multiple classes of interest. Furthermore, GVEX generates high-quality explanation subgraphs by identifying important nodes exploiting factual and counterfactual properties and by computing their aggregated influence on the remaining nodes following the GNN message passing paradigm. Lastly, GVEX performs a summarize step on top of lower-tier explanation structures to generate higher-tier graph patterns that offer direct access for users with (domain-aware) queries. Our demonstration will highlight (1) a novel two-tier explanation structure called explanation views, consisting of graph patterns and a set of explanation subgraphs, which provide high-quality explanations for GNNs; (2) the system's intuitive GUI facilitates user interaction to configure personalized settings, e.g., classes of interest and explanation size, and compare with other explanation algorithms; (3) GVEX generates queryable explanations, making it easy for human experts to access and inspect with domain knowledge. Our demonstration video is at: https://youtu.be/q9d7ldulIuQ.
items.abstract_zh:
由于图神经网络 (GNN) 的“黑盒”性质，解释它们的行为变得至关重要，特别是在图分类等分析任务的背景下。当前的方法仅限于为单个实例或特定类标签提供解释，并且可能返回难以访问或直接查询的大型解释结构。在本文中，我们提出了 GVEX [1]（GNN EX planation 的 G raph V views）——我们开发的系统是为了基于图形视图为 GNN 提供用户友好、交互式和可配置的解释。

GVEX 提供了一个配置组件，使用户能够轻松地从不同类别中选择所需数量的重要节点，从而生成针对多个感兴趣类别的解释。此外，GVEX 通过利用事实和反事实属性来识别重要节点，并按照 GNN 消息传递范式计算它们对其余节点的聚合影响，从而生成高质量的解释子图。最后，GVEX 在较低层解释结构之上执行汇总步骤，以生成较高层图形模式，为具有（域感知）查询的用户提供直接访问。我们的演示将重点介绍（1）一种新颖的两层解释结构，称为解释视图，由图模式和一组解释子图组成，为 GNN 提供高质量的解释； (2)系统直观的GUI便于用户交互配置个性化设置，例如兴趣类别和解释大小，并与其他解释算法进行比较； （3）GVEX生成可查询的解释，使人类专家可以轻松地使用领域知识进行访问和检查。我们的演示视频位于：https://youtu.be/q9d7ldulIuQ。

items.title: Machine Learning for Databases: Foundations, Paradigms, and Open problems.
items.abstract: This tutorial delves into the burgeoning field of Machine Learning for Databases (ML4DB), highlighting its recent progress and the challenges impeding its integration into industrial-grade database management systems. We systematically explore three key themes: the exploration of foundations in ML4DB and their potential for diverse applications, the two paradigms in ML4DB, i.e., using machine learning as a replacement versus enhancement of traditional database components, and the critical open challenges such as improving model efficiency and addressing data shifts. Through an in-depth analysis, including a survey of recent works in major database conferences, this tutorial encapsulates the current state of ML4DB, as well as charts a roadmap for its future development and wider adoption in practical database environments.
items.abstract_zh: 本教程深入探讨了新兴的数据库机器学习 (ML4DB) 领域，重点介绍了其最新进展以及阻碍其集成到工业级数据库管理系统中的挑战。我们系统地探讨了三个关键主题：探索 ML4DB 的基础及其在不同应用中的潜力、ML4DB 中的两种范式，即使用机器学习作为传统数据库组件的替代与增强，以及提高模型效率和解决数据转移等关键的开放挑战。通过深入分析，包括对主要数据库会议近期工作的调查，本教程概括了 ML4DB 的现状，并为其未来的发展和在实际数据库环境中更广泛采用绘制了路线图。

items.title: Fourth International Workshop on Big Data in Emergent Distributed Environments (BiDEDE).
items.abstract: Emergent distributed environments going beyond cloud computing offer new kinds of processing for massive and heterogeneous data, but also pose new challenges for data management. These challenges include among others: load balancing, dynamic data allocation on computing nodes, fault tolerance, handling low quality data, data and processes replication, resource provisioning, buffer management, query processing and optimization on different types of hardware, transaction management, and minimization of power usage. The BiDEDE workshop addresses these and other challenges related to building post-cloud distributed environments for data processing.
items.abstract_zh: 超越云计算的新兴分布式环境为海量异构数据提供了新的处理方式，但也给数据管理带来了新的挑战。这些挑战包括：负载平衡、计算节点上的动态数据分配、容错、处理低质量数据、数据和进程复制、资源配置、缓冲区管理、不同类型硬件上的查询处理和优化、事务管理以及功耗最小化。 BiDEDE 研讨会解决了与构建后云分布式数据处理环境相关的这些挑战和其他挑战。

items.title: AI for Systems.
items.abstract: In the last decade, data management has been steadily moving to-wards public clouds, turning DBMS "builders" into "operators". The combined scale and vertical integration gives us the opportunity to optimize systems holistically using a telemetry-based feedback loop. These events aligned with fast advances in the field of Data Science to create the perfect conditions for an explosion of "ML for Systems" solutions. More recently, massive improvements in Large Language Models (LLMs) has introduced another intriguing element into the mix. In this panel, we explore questions around the practical adoption and robustness of ML for Systems solutions, as well as the potential role for LLMs, spanning very concrete engineering considerations and very open-ended "future of this industry" type thinking.
items.abstract_zh: 在过去的十年中，数据管理一直在稳步向公共云发展，将 DBMS“构建者”转变为“运营者”。规模和垂直整合的结合使我们有机会使用基于遥测的反馈循环来整体优化系统。这些事件与数据科学领域的快速发展相结合，为“系统机器学习”解决方案的爆炸式增长创造了完美的条件。最近，大型语言模型（LLM）的巨大改进引入了另一个有趣的元素。在这个小组中，我们探讨了有关系统机器学习解决方案的实际采用和稳健性的问题，以及法学硕士的潜在作用，涵盖非常具体的工程考虑因素和非常开放的“这个行业的未来”类型的思维。

items.title: ASQP-RL Demo: Learning Approximation Sets for Exploratory Queries.
items.abstract: We demonstrate the Approximate Selection Query Processing (ASQP-RL) system, which uses Reinforcement Learning to select a subset of a large external dataset to process locally in a notebook during data exploration. Given a query workload over an external database and notebook memory size, the system translates the workload to select-project-join (non-aggregate) queries and finds a subset of each relation such that the data subset - called the approximation set - fits into the notebook memory and maximizes query result quality. The data subset can then be loaded into the notebook, and rapidly queried by the analyst. Our demonstration shows how ASQP-RL can be used during data exploration and achieve comparable results to external queries over the large dataset at significantly reduced query times. It also shows how ASQP-RL can be used for aggregation queries, achieving surprisingly good results compared to state-of-the-art techniques.
items.abstract_zh: 我们演示了近似选择查询处理 (ASQP-RL) 系统，该系统使用强化学习来选择大型外部数据集的子集，以便在数据探索期间在笔记本中进行本地处理。给定外部数据库上的查询工作负载和笔记本内存大小，系统会将工作负载转换为选择项目连接（非聚合）查询，并找到每个关系的子集，以便数据子集（称为近似集）适合笔记本内存并最大化查询结果质量。然后可以将数据子集加载到笔记本中，并由分析师快速查询。我们的演示展示了如何在数据探索期间使用 ASQP-RL，并在显着减少查询时间的情况下，在大型数据集上获得与外部查询相当的结果。它还展示了如何将 ASQP-RL 用于聚合查询，与最先进的技术相比，取得了令人惊讶的良好结果。

items.title: TimeCloth: Fast Point-in-Time Database Recovery in The Cloud.
items.abstract:
In recent years, we have noted the frequent occurrence of user-triggered database recoveries in the cloud. In contrast to traditional failure-triggered recovery scenarios, they come with unique consumer-centric challenges. Unfortunately, current solutions prove inefficient, falling short either in meeting our customers' requirements or due to their close integration with the native recovery support of the underlying database engines.

In this work, we present TimeCloth, a generic cloud-native recovery mechanism that achieves sublinear recovery time while meeting the specific needs of our customers. It comprises a recovery module optimized for fine-grained point-in-time recoveries and an import module enabling nearly instantaneous access to remote tables. The recovery module performs fast log filtering, parallelizes replay of non-conflicting log records and coalesce log records to reduce the volume of replay work. The import module implements a transparent FUSE-based lazy loading mechanism as well as a smart prefetcher to achieve good access performance for remote tables. Collectively, they significantly accelerate user-triggered recoveries in the cloud. TimeCloth is launched in production at Alibaba Cloud for about 15 months. We have witnesses a reduction in RTO among our customers by 44% on average and sometimes up to 92%.
items.abstract_zh:
近年来，我们注意到云中用户触发的数据库恢复频繁发生。与传统的故障触发恢复场景相比，它们面临着以消费者为中心的独特挑战。不幸的是，当前的解决方案被证明效率低下，要么无法满足客户的需求，要么由于它们与底层数据库引擎的本机恢复支持的紧密集成而无法实现。

在这项工作中，我们提出了 TimeCloth，这是一种通用的云原生恢复机制，可以实现亚线性恢复时间，同时满足客户的特定需求。它包括一个针对细粒度时间点恢复进行优化的恢复模块和一个支持几乎即时访问远程表的导入模块。恢复模块执行快速日志过滤，并行重放非冲突日志记录并合并日志记录以减少重放工作量。导入模块实现了基于FUSE的透明延迟加载机制以及智能预取器，以实现对远程表的良好访问性能。总的来说，它们显着加速了云中用户触发的恢复。 TimeCloth 在阿里云上投入生产约 15 个月。我们见证了客户的 RTO 平均减少了 44%，有时甚至高达 92%。

items.title: IDE: A System for Iterative Mislabel Detection.
items.abstract:
While machine learning techniques, especially deep neural networks, have shown remarkable success in various applications, their performance is adversely affected by label errors in training data. Acquiring high-quality annotated data is both costly and time-consuming in real-world scenarios, requiring extensive human annotation and verification. Consequently, many industry-applied models are trained over data containing substantial noise, significantly degrading the performance of these models.

To address this critical issue, we demonstrate IDE, a novel system that iteratively detects mislabeled instances and repairs the wrong labels. Specifically, IDE leverages the early loss observation and influence-based verification to iteratively identify mislabeled instances. When the mislabeled instances are obtained in each iteration, IDE will repair their labels to enhance detection accuracy for subsequent iterations. The framework automatically determines the termination point when the early loss is no longer effective. For uncertain instances, it generates pseudo labels to train a binary classification model, leveraging the model's generalization ability to make the final decision. With a real-life scenario, we demonstrate that IDE produces high-quality training data by effective mislabel detection and repair.
items.abstract_zh:
虽然机器学习技术，特别是深度神经网络，在各种应用中取得了显着的成功，但它们的性能受到训练数据中标签错误的不利影响。在现实场景中，获取高质量的注释数据既昂贵又耗时，需要大量的人工注释和验证。因此，许多行业应用的模型都是在包含大量噪声的数据上进行训练的，从而显着降低了这些模型的性能。

为了解决这个关键问题，我们演示了 IDE，这是一种新颖的系统，可以迭代地检测错误标记的实例并修复错误的标签。具体来说，IDE 利用早期丢失观察和基于影响的验证来迭代识别错误标记的实例。当每次迭代中获得错误标记的实例时，IDE将修复其标签，以提高后续迭代的检测准确性。当早期损失不再有效时，框架会自动确定终止点。对于不确定的情况，它生成伪标签来训练二元分类模型，利用模型的泛化能力做出最终决策。通过现实场景，我们证明 IDE 通过有效的错误标签检测和修复来生成高质量的训练数据。

items.title: Demonstration of Ver: View Discovery in the Wild.
items.abstract: We demonstrate Ver¹ [10], a data discovery system that identifies project-join views over large repositories of tables that do not contain join path information. Ver solves both the technical (scale and search) and human (semantic ambiguity, navigating a large number of results) problems of view discovery.
items.abstract_zh: 我们演示了 Ver1 [10]，这是一个数据发现系统，可识别不包含连接路径信息的大型表存储库上的项目连接视图。 Ver 解决了视图发现的技术（规模和搜索）和人类（语义模糊、导航大量结果）问题。

items.title: Automated Multidimensional Data Layouts in Amazon Redshift.
items.abstract:
Analytic data systems typically use data layouts to improve the performance of scanning and filtering data. Common data layout techniques include single-column sort keys, compound sort keys, and more complex multidimensional data layouts such as the Z-order. An appropriately-selected data layout over a table, in combination with metadata such as zone maps, enables the system to skip irrelevant data blocks when scanning the table, which reduces the amount of data scanned and improves query performance.

In this paper, we introduce Multidimensional Data Layouts (MDDL), a new data layout technique which outperforms existing data layout techniques for query workloads with repetitive scan filters. Unlike existing data layout approaches, which typically sort tables based on columns, MDDL sorts tables based on a collection of predicates, which enables a much higher degree of specialization to the user's workload. We additionally introduce an algorithm for automatically learning the best MDDL for each table based on telemetry collected from the historical workload. We implemented MDDL within Amazon Redshift. Benchmarks on internal datasets and workloads show that MDDL achieves up to 85% reduction in end-to-end workload runtime compared to using traditional column-based data layout techniques. MDDL is, to the best of our knowledge, the first data layout technique in a commercial product that sorts based on predicates and automatically learns the best predicates.
items.abstract_zh:
分析数据系统通常使用数据布局来提高扫描和过滤数据的性能。常见的数据布局技术包括单列排序键、复合排序键以及更复杂的多维数据布局（例如 Z 顺序）。适当选择表上的数据布局，结合区域映射等元数据，使系统在扫描表时跳过不相关的数据块，从而减少扫描的数据量并提高查询性能。

在本文中，我们介绍了多维数据布局（MDDL），这是一种新的数据布局技术，对于具有重复扫描过滤器的查询工作负载，其性能优于现有的数据布局技术。与通常根据列对表进行排序的现有数据布局方法不同，MDDL 根据谓词集合对表进行排序，这使得用户的工作负载能够实现更高程度的专业化。我们还引入了一种算法，用于根据从历史工作负载收集的遥测数据自动学习每个表的最佳 MDDL。我们在 Amazon Redshift 中实施了 MDDL。内部数据集和工作负载的基准测试表明，与使用传统的基于列的数据布局技术相比，MDDL 可将端到端工作负载运行时间减少高达 85%。据我们所知，MDDL 是商业产品中第一个基于谓词排序并自动学习最佳谓词的数据布局技术。

items.title: The Journey to a Knowledgeable Assistant with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
items.abstract:
For decades, multiple communities (Database, Information Retrieval, Natural Language Processing, Data Mining, AI) have pursued the mission of providing the right information at the right time. Efforts span web search, data integration, knowledge graphs, question answering. Recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in comprehending and generating human language, revolutionizing techniques in every front. However, their inherent limitations such as factual inaccuracies and hallucinations make LLMs less suitable for creating knowledgeable and trustworthy assistants.

This talk describes our journey in building a knowledgeable AI assistant by harnessing LLM techniques. We start with our findings from a comprehensive set of experiments to assess LLM reliability in answering factual questions and analyze performance variations across different knowledge types. Next, we describe our federated Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system that integrates external information from both the web and knowledge graphs for trustworthy text generation on real-time topics like stocks and sports, as well as on torso-to-tail entities like local restaurants. Additionally, we brief our explorations on extending our techniques towards multi-modal, contextualized, and personalized Q&A. We will share our techniques, our findings, and the path forward, high- lighting how we are leveraging and advancing the decades of work in this area.
items.abstract_zh:
几十年来，多个社区（数据库、信息检索、自然语言处理、数据挖掘、人工智能）一直追求在正确的时间提供正确的信息的使命。努力涵盖网络搜索、数据集成、知识图谱、问题解答。大型语言模型 (LLM) 的最新进展展示了在理解和生成人类语言方面的卓越能力，彻底改变了各个领域的技术。然而，其固有的局限性，例如事实不准确和幻觉，使得法学硕士不太适合培养知识渊博、值得信赖的助手。

本次演讲描述了我们利用法学硕士技术构建知识渊博的人工智能助手的历程。我们从一系列全面的实验中得出结论，评估法学硕士在回答事实问题方面的可靠性，并分析不同知识类型的表现差异。接下来，我们描述我们的联合检索增强生成（RAG）系统，该系统集成了来自网络和知识图的外部信息，以生成关于股票和体育等实时主题以及当地餐馆等从躯干到尾部的实体的可信文本。此外，我们还简要介绍了将我们的技术扩展到多模式、情境化和个性化问答的探索。我们将分享我们的技术、我们的发现和前进的道路，重点介绍我们如何利用和推进该领域数十年的工作。

items.title: Multi-Backend Zonal Statistics Execution with Raven.
items.abstract: The recent explosion in the number and size of spatial remote sensing datasets from satellite missions creates new opportunities for data-driven approaches in domains such as climate change monitoring and disaster management. These approaches typically involve a feature engineering step that summarizes remote sensing pixel data located within zones of interest defined by another spatial dataset, an operation called zonal statistics. Although several spatial systems support zonal statistics operations, they differ significantly in terms of interfaces, architectures, and algorithms, making it hard for users to select the best system for a specific workload. To address this limitation, we propose Raven, a zonal statistics framework that provides users with a unified interface across multiple execution backends, while facilitating easy benchmarking and comparisons across systems. This demonstration showcases Raven 's multi-backend execution environment, domain-specific declarative language, optimization techniques, and benchmarking capabilities.
items.abstract_zh: 最近，卫星任务空间遥感数据集的数量和规模呈爆炸式增长，为气候变化监测和灾害管理等领域的数据驱动方法创造了新的机遇。这些方法通常涉及特征工程步骤，该步骤总结位于由另一个空间数据集定义的感兴趣区域内的遥感像素数据，这种操作称为区域统计。尽管多个空间系统支持分区统计操作，但它们在接口、架构和算法方面存在显着差异，使得用户很难为特定工作负载选择最佳系统。为了解决这个限制，我们提出了 Raven，一个区域统计框架，为用户提供跨多个执行后端的统一界面，同时促进跨系统的轻松基准测试和比较。该演示展示了 Raven 的多后端执行环境、特定领域的声明性语言、优化技术和基准测试功能。

items.title: Vortex: A Stream-oriented Storage Engine For Big Data Analytics.
items.abstract: Organizations are increasingly looking for ways to simplify collection and transformation of vast amounts of data collected from a highly connected internet. Data analytics over continuous streams of data enables interactive applications and reduces time to insights. Traditionally, streaming data collection and analysis has been either achieved by building systems, or using data warehouses built for batch processing. In this paper, we present Vortex, a storage engine that we built inside Google BigQuery to support real-time analytics. Vortex is a streaming-first storage system that supports both streaming and batch data analytics. Today, BigQuery uses Vortex to support petabyte scale data ingestion with sub-second data freshness and query latency.
items.abstract_zh: 组织越来越多地寻找方法来简化从高度连接的互联网收集的大量数据的收集和转换。对连续数据流的数据分析支持交互式应用程序并缩短获得洞察的时间。传统上，流数据收集和分析是通过构建系统或使用为批处理构建的数据仓库来实现的。在本文中，我们介绍了 Vortex，这是我们在 Google BigQuery 内部构建的一种存储引擎，用于支持实时分析。 Vortex 是一种流优先存储系统，支持流式和批量数据分析。如今，BigQuery 使用 Vortex 支持 PB 级数据提取，并具有亚秒级数据新鲜度和查询延迟。

items.title: SmartNICs in the Cloud: The Why, What and How of In-network Processing for Data-Intensive Applications.
items.abstract: In modern datacenters and clouds, Resource Disaggregation has been adopted as a way of offering scalability and efficient resource utilization for large-scale applications. Provisioning CPU, memory, and storage resources independently for distributed data-intensive applications is a great enabler but it also brings challenges, especially in the form of networking and processing overhead. To reduce this overhead and to make disaggregation-related tasks significantly more efficient, cloud providers are offloading these to the network, i.e., to Smart Network Interface Cards (SmartNICs) and Smart Switches. Beyond this specific use-case, the presence of such programmable hardware in commercial clouds creates future opportunities for offloading application-level operations, e.g., parts of SQL queries or ML pipelines. To map out this exciting space, in this tutorial we take a detailed look at SmartNICs, explaining how they work, giving examples of what they are good for, and highlighting how they can best be utilized to make future data-intensive and distributed systems more efficient.
