items.abstract_zh: 在现代数据中心和云中，资源分解已被用作为大规模应用程序提供可扩展性和高效资源利用的一种方式。为分布式数据密集型应用程序独立配置 CPU、内存和存储资源是一个很好的推动因素，但它也带来了挑战，特别是在网络和处理开销方面。为了减少这种开销并显着提高与分解相关的任务的效率，云提供商正在将这些任务卸载到网络，即智能网络接口卡 (SmartNIC) 和智能交换机。除了这个特定的用例之外，商业云中此类可编程硬件的存在为卸载应用程序级操作（例如部分 SQL 查询或 ML 管道）创造了未来的机会。为了描绘这个令人兴奋的空间，在本教程中，我们详细介绍了 SmartNIC，解释了它们的工作原理，举例说明了它们的用途，并强调了如何最好地利用它们来提高未来数据密集型和分布式系统的效率。

items.title: Eighth Workshop on Human-In-the-Loop Data Analytics (HILDA).
items.abstract: HILDA brings together researchers and practitioners to exchange ideas and results on human-data interaction. It explores how data management and analysis can be made more effective when taking into account the people who design and build these processes as well as those who are impacted by their results. Following the past two years, we plan to continue to focus on this year's workshop on early-stage research that is promising and exciting, which includes pairing each accepted paper with a mentor. The theme for this edition of the workshop is HILDA and Large Language Models. However, the workshop is not limited to this theme and other topics are also of interest. In this summary, we describe the workshop, its main focus areas, our review and mentorship plan as well as the keynote and panel sessions.
items.abstract_zh: HILDA 将研究人员和从业者聚集在一起，交流有关人类数据交互的想法和结果。它探讨了在考虑设计和构建这些流程的人员以及受其结果影响的人员的情况下，如何使数据管理和分析更加有效。继过去两年之后，我们计划继续关注今年的早期研究研讨会，这些研究前景光明且令人兴奋，其中包括为每篇被接受的论文与导师配对。本期研讨会的主题是 HILDA 和大型语言模型。不过，研讨会并不局限于这个主题，其他话题也很有趣。在本摘要中，我们描述了研讨会、其主要重点领域、我们的审查和指导计划以及主题演讲和小组会议。

items.title: Responsible Sharing of Spatiotemporal Data.
items.abstract: There is a growing need for responsible spatiotemporal data sharing in our daily lives. Applications such as connected vehicles and mobile advertising are currently undergoing a significant digital shift, demanding new standards for privacy-aware solutions and the integration of machine learning technologies. In this tutorial, we present the concepts and challenges encountered when maximizing the utility of spatiotemporal data while enforcing rigorous privacy and security measures. We review modern data sharing mechanisms that provide stakeholders with the power to establish precise terms for the usage and sharing of their data, secured by a robust data infrastructure. We will explore how such sharing mechanisms interplay with complex privacy stipulations and advanced spatiotemporal analytics. Attendees will leave with a comprehensive understanding of how to navigate the delicate balance of spatiotemporal data usage, paving the way for innovation in privacy and compliance methodologies across various industries.
items.abstract_zh: 我们的日常生活中对负责任的时空数据共享的需求日益增长。联网车辆和移动广告等应用目前正在经历重大的数字化转变，需要隐私意识解决方案和机器学习技术集成的新标准。在本教程中，我们介绍了在执行严格的隐私和安全措施的同时最大化时空数据的效用时遇到的概念和挑战。我们回顾了现代数据共享机制，这些机制为利益相关者提供了建立精确数据使用和共享条款的权力，并由强大的数据基础设施保障。我们将探讨这种共享机制如何与复杂的隐私规定和先进的时空分析相互作用。与会者将全面了解如何驾驭时空数据使用的微妙平衡，为各行业隐私和合规方法的创新铺平道路。

items.title: Demonstrating λ-Tune: Exploiting Large Language Models for Workload-Adaptive Database System Tuning.
items.abstract: We demonstrate λ-Tune, a tool that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) for automated, workload-adaptive database system tuning. λ-Tune harnesses the ability of LLMs to process and comprehend arbitrary textual data in a zero-shot manner, employing a workload-adaptive optimization approach. Given a database system, the hardware specifications, and a set of queries, λ-Tune generates prompts to retrieve configuration recommendations for the tuning knobs and the physical design, tailored to the specific system and workload. Our framework utilizes a workload compression approach that extracts and includes in the prompt only the most insightful workload characteristics, while the prompt size can be adjusted by a user-defined token budget. Utilizing the zero-shot capabilities of LLMs, λ-Tune outperforms other LLM and machine learning-enhanced database tuning baselines, which rely on time-consuming tuning and training phases, as well as expensive hardware, such as GPUs. During demonstration, users will be able to experiment with λ-Tune to tune Postgres and MySQL, as well as explore and modify the configurations retrieved by the LLM in an interactive way through λ-Tune's user interface.
items.abstract_zh: 我们演示了 λ-Tune，这是一种利用大型语言模型 (LLM) 进行自动化、工作负载自适应数据库系统调优的工具。 λ-Tune 利用法学硕士以零样本方式处理和理解任意文本数据的能力，采用工作负载自适应优化方法。给定数据库系统、硬件规格和一组查询，λ-Tune 会生成提示来检索针对特定系统和工作负载定制的调整旋钮和物理设计的配置建议。我们的框架采用工作负载压缩方法，仅提取最有洞察力的工作负载特征并将其包含在提示中，而提示大小可以通过用户定义的令牌预算进行调整。利用 LLM 的零样本功能，λ-Tune 的性能优于其他 LLM 和机器学习增强型数据库调优基线，这些基线依赖于耗时的调优和训练阶段以及昂贵的硬件（例如 GPU）。在演示过程中，用户将能够尝试使用 λ-Tune 来调整 Postgres 和 MySQL，并通过 λ-Tune 的用户界面以交互方式探索和修改 LLM 检索到的配置。

items.title: Demonstrating Nexus for Correlation Discovery over Collections of Spatio-Temporal Tabular Data.
items.abstract: Causal analysis is crucial for understanding cause-and-effect relationships in observed data to inform better decisions. However, conducting precise causal analysis on observational data is usually impractical, so domain experts often begin their exploration by identifying correlations. In this paper, we demonstrate Nexus, a system that aligns tabular datasets across space and time, handles missing data, and identifies correlations deemed "interesting", facilitating the exploration of causal relationships.
items.abstract_zh: 因果分析对于理解观察数据中的因果关系以提供更好的决策至关重要。然而，对观测数据进行精确的因果分析通常是不切实际的，因此领域专家通常通过识别相关性来开始探索。在本文中，我们演示了 Nexus，这是一个跨空间和时间对齐表格数据集、处理缺失数据并识别被认为“有趣”的相关性的系统，从而促进因果关系的探索。

items.title: Tenth International Workshop on Testing Database Systems (DBTest).
items.abstract: With the ever-increasing amount of data stored and processed and ever-evolving hardware technology, there is not only an ongoing need for testing database management systems but also data-intensive systems in general. Reviving the previous success of nine previous workshops, the goal of the Workshop on Testing Database Systems (DBTest) 2024 is to bring researchers and practitioners from academia and industry together to discuss key problems and ideas related to testing database systems and applications. The long-term objective of our efforts is to reduce the cost and time required to test and tune data management and processing products so that users and vendors can spend more time and energy on actual innovations.
items.abstract_zh: 随着存储和处理的数据量不断增加以及硬件技术的不断发展，不仅需要持续测试数据库管理系统，而且还需要测试一般的数据密集型系统。 2024 年数据库系统测试研讨会 (DBTest) 的目标是让学术界和工业界的研究人员和从业者齐聚一堂，讨论与测试数据库系统和应用程序相关的关键问题和想法，延续之前九场研讨会的成功经验。我们努力的长期目标是减少测试和调整数据管理和处理产品所需的成本和时间，以便用户和供应商可以将更多的时间和精力用于实际创新。

items.title: SIMDified Data Processing - Foundations, Abstraction, and Advanced Techniques.
items.abstract: Query execution techniques in database systems are constantly adapting to novel hardware features in order to improve query performance, in particular for analytical queries. In the last decade, the Single Instruction Multiple Data (SIMD) paradigm was established as a state-of-the-art approach to increase the single-query performance by parallelizing in-core. Such SIMD capabilities are constantly increasing in modern processors by more comprehensive SIMD instruction set extensions. Therefore, this tutorial will provide an up-to-date overview about SIMDified query processing concepts. Conceptually, this tutorial is divided into three parts. In the first part, we lay the foundations by describing what SIMD is, what it was introduced for, and how it is traditionally used to accelerate query processing. The subsequent two parts focus on novel developments in this area. While the second part of this tutorial is dedicated to an abstraction layer to handle the increasing heterogeneity in the SIMD hardware landscape, the third part provides a survey of advanced techniques to address upcoming challenges such as wider and flexibly-sized SIMD registers.
items.abstract_zh: 数据库系统中的查询执行技术不断适应新的硬件功能，以提高查询性能，特别是分析查询。在过去的十年中，单指令多数据 (SIMD) 范例被建立为一种最先进的方法，通过内核并行化来提高单查询性能。通过更全面的 SIMD 指令集扩展，现代处理器中的这种 SIMD 功能不断增强。因此，本教程将提供有关 SIMDified 查询处理概念的最新概述。从概念上讲，本教程分为三个部分。在第一部分中，我们通过描述 SIMD 是什么、引入它的用途以及传统上如何使用它来加速查询处理来奠定基础。接下来的两部分重点介绍该领域的新发展。虽然本教程的第二部分致力于抽象层来处理 SIMD 硬件环境中日益增长的异构性，但第三部分提供了对先进技术的调查，以应对即将到来的挑战，例如更宽和灵活大小的 SIMD 寄存器。

items.title: GRADES-NDA'24: 7th Joint Workshop on Graph Data Management Experiences & Systems (GRADES) and Network Data Analytics (NDA).
items.abstract: GRADES-NDA is the premier workshop series on graph data management and analytics that aims to bring together researchers from academia, industry, and governmental organizations. GRADES-NDA'24 is a forum for discussing recent advances in (large-scale) graph data management and analytics systems, as well as proposing and discussing novel methods and techniques for addressing domain-specific challenges. In 2024, GRADES-NDA is in its seventh edition.
items.abstract_zh: GRADES-NDA 是有关图形数据管理和分析的顶级研讨会系列，旨在汇集来自学术界、工业界和政府组织的研究人员。 GRADES-NDA'24 是一个讨论（大规模）图数据管理和分析系统最新进展的论坛，以及提出和讨论解决特定领域挑战的新方法和技术。 2024 年，GRADES-NDA 已进入第七版。

items.title: GraphScope Flex: LEGO-like Graph Computing Stack.
items.abstract: Graph computing has become increasingly crucial in processing large-scale graph data, with numerous systems developed for this purpose. Two years ago, we introduced GraphScope as a system addressing a wide array of graph computing needs, including graph traversal, analytics, and learning in one system. Since its inception, GraphScope has achieved significant technological advancements and gained widespread adoption across various industries. However, one key lesson from this journey has been understanding the limitations of a "one-size-fits-all" approach, especially when dealing with the diversity of programming interfaces, applications, and data storage formats in graph computing. In response to these challenges, we present GraphScope Flex, the next iteration of GraphScope. GraphScope Flex is designed to be both resource-efficient and cost-effective, while also providing flexibility and user-friendliness through its LEGO-like modularity. This paper explores the architectural innovations and fundamental design principles of GraphScope Flex, all of which are direct outcomes of the lessons learned during our ongoing development process. We validate the adaptability and efficiency of GraphScope Flex with extensive evaluations on synthetic and real-world datasets. The results show that GraphScope Flex achieves 2.4× throughput and up to 55.7× speedup over other systems on the LDBC Social Network and Graphalytics benchmarks, respectively. Furthermore, GraphScope Flex accomplishes up to a 2,400× performance gain in real-world applications, demonstrating its proficiency across a wide range of graph computing scenarios with increased effectiveness.
items.abstract_zh: 图计算在处理大规模图数据方面变得越来越重要，为此开发了许多系统。两年前，我们推出了 GraphScope 作为一个系统，可以满足广泛的图计算需求，包括在一个系统中进行图遍历、分析和学习。自成立以来，GraphScope 取得了重大的技术进步，并在各个行业得到了广泛采用。然而，这一旅程的一个重要教训是了解“一刀切”方法的局限性，特别是在处理图计算中编程接口、应用程序和数据存储格式的多样性时。为了应对这些挑战，我们推出了 GraphScope Flex，这是 GraphScope 的下一代产品。 GraphScope Flex 的设计既具有资源效率又具有成本效益，同时还通过其类似乐高的模块化提供灵活性和用户友好性。本文探讨了 GraphScope Flex 的架构创新和基本设计原则，所有这些都是我们在持续开发过程中吸取的经验教训的直接结果。我们通过对合成数据集和真实数据集的广泛评估来验证 GraphScope Flex 的适应性和效率。结果表明，与其他系统相比，GraphScope Flex 在 LDBC 社交网络和 Graphalytics 基准测试中分别实现了 2.4 倍的吞吐量和高达 55.7 倍的加速。此外，GraphScope Flex 在实际应用中实现了高达 2,400 倍的性能提升，展示了其在各种图形计算场景中的熟练程度以及更高的效率。

items.title: Responsible Model Selection with Virny and VirnyView.
items.abstract: In this demonstration, we present a comprehensive software library for model auditing and responsible model selection, called Virny, along with an interactive tool called VirnyView. Our library is modular and extensible, it implements a rich set of performance and fairness metrics, including novel metrics that quantify and compare model stability and uncertainty, and enables performance analysis based on multiple sensitive attributes, and their intersections. The Virny library and the VirnyView tool are available at https://github.com/DataResponsibly/Virny and https://r-ai.co/VirnyView.
items.abstract_zh: 在本次演示中，我们展示了一个名为 Virny 的用于模型审核和负责任模型选择的综合软件库，以及一个名为 VirnyView 的交互式工具。我们的库是模块化和可扩展的，它实现了一组丰富的性能和公平性指标，包括量化和比较模型稳定性和不确定性的新颖指标，并支持基于多个敏感属性及其交集的性能分析。 Virny 库和 VirnyView 工具可在 https://github.com/DataResponsbilly/Virny 和 https://r-ai.co/VirnyView 上获取。

items.title: Querying Graph Databases at Scale.
items.abstract: The tutorial provides an in-depth overview of recent advances in algorithms and data structures for processing graph database queries. The focus will be on scalable algorithms that have been demonstrated to work over real world knowledge graphs. We will also present detailed performance comparisons of classical and recent algorithms. The tutorial will be divided into four sections. The first section will motivate the use of graph databases for querying knowledge graphs, and will introduce the attendees to graph data models and the query language landscape. The second section will discuss how to efficiently evaluate graph patterns, introducing the worst-case optimal join techniques and comparing them to classical join algorithms. The third section will discuss techniques for efficiently evaluating path queries and for constructing compact representations of potentially exponential sets of paths. In the final section we will introduce recent advances in compressed data structures that ease the high memory requirements of worst-case optimal join algorithms and also provide a template for evaluating path queries in a highly optimised manner.
items.abstract_zh: 本教程深入概述了用于处理图数据库查询的算法和数据结构的最新进展。重点将放在已被证明可以在现实世界知识图谱上工作的可扩展算法。我们还将展示经典算法和最新算法的详细性能比较。本教程将分为四个部分。第一部分将促进使用图数据库来查询知识图，并向与会者介绍图数据模型和查询语言环境。第二部分将讨论如何有效地评估图模式，介绍最坏情况的最佳连接技术并将其与经典连接算法进行比较。第三部分将讨论有效评估路径查询和构建潜在指数路径集的紧凑表示的技术。在最后一节中，我们将介绍压缩数据结构的最新进展，这些进展可以缓解最坏情况最优连接算法的高内存需求，并提供用于以高度优化的方式评估路径查询的模板。

items.title: Eighth Workshop on Data Management for End-to-End Machine Learning (DEEM).
items.abstract: The DEEM'24 workshop (Data Management for End-to-End Machine Learning) is held on Sunday June 9th, in conjunction with SIGMOD/PODS 2024. DEEM brings together researchers and practitioners at the intersection of applied machine learning, data management and systems research, with the goal to discuss the arising data management issues in ML application scenarios. The workshop solicits regular research papers (8 pages) describing preliminary and ongoing research results, including industrial experience reports of end-to-end ML deployments, related to DEEM topics. In addition, DEEM 2023 has a category for short papers (4 pages) as a forum for sharing interesting use cases, problems, datasets, benchmarks, visionary ideas, system designs, preliminary results, and descriptions of system components and tools related to end-to-end ML pipelines. This year, the workshop received 16 high-quality submissions on diverse topics relevant to DEEM, of which 8 regular papers and 8 short papers.
items.abstract_zh: DEEM'24 研讨会（端到端机器学习的数据管理）于 6 月 9 日星期日与 SIGMOD/PODS 2024 一起举行。DEEM 汇集了应用机器学习、数据管理和系统研究交叉领域的研究人员和从业者，旨在讨论 ML 应用场景中出现的数据管理问题。该研讨会征集定期研究论文（8 页），描述初步和正在进行的研究成果，包括与 DEEM 主题相关的端到端 ML 部署的行业经验报告。此外，DEEM 2023 还有一个短论文类别（4 页）作为论坛，用于分享有趣的用例、问题、数据集、基准、远见卓识、系统设计、初步结果以及与端到端 ML 管道相关的系统组件和工具的描述。今年，研讨会收到了 16 篇与 DEEM 相关的不同主题的高质量论文，其中 8 篇常规论文和 8 篇短论文。

items.title: Measures in SQL.
items.abstract:
SQL has attained widespread adoption, but Business Intelligence tools still use their own higher level languages based upon a multidimensional paradigm. Composable calculations are what is missing from SQL, and we propose a new kind of column, called a measure, that attaches a calculation to a table. Like regular tables, tables with measures are composable and closed when used in queries.

SQL-with-measures has the power, conciseness and reusability of multidimensional languages but retains SQL semantics. Measure invocations can be expanded in place to simple, clear SQL.

To define the evaluation semantics for measures, we introduce context-sensitive expressions (a way to evaluate multidimensional expressions that is consistent with existing SQL semantics), a concept called evaluation context, and several operations for setting and modifying the evaluation context.
items.abstract_zh:
SQL 已得到广泛采用，但商业智能工具仍然使用自己的基于多维范式的高级语言。可组合计算是 SQL 中所缺少的，我们提出了一种新的列，称为度量，它将计算附加到表中。与常规表一样，具有度量的表在查询中使用时是可组合和关闭的。

SQL-with-measures 具有多维语言的强大、简洁和可重用性，但保留了 SQL 语义。测量调用可以就地扩展为简单、清晰的 SQL。

为了定义度量的求值语义，我们引入了上下文相关表达式（一种与现有 SQL 语义一致的求值多维表达式的方法）、一个称为求值上下文的概念以及用于设置和修改求值上下文的几个操作。

items.title: Native Cloud Object Storage in Db2 Warehouse: Implementing a Fast and Cost-Efficient Cloud Storage Architecture.
items.abstract: Database systems built on traditional storage subsystems typically store their data in small blocks referred to as data pages (commonly sized in a multiple of 4KB for historical reasons). These traditional storage subsystems, for example network attached block storage, were designed for efficient random-access I/O patterns at the block level, and the block size is usually configurable by the application based on its needs. For large scale analytic databases in cloud environments, these traditional storage subsystems are not cost effective when compared to cloud object storage, and database systems that exploit them risk becoming uncompetitive. This paper describes the modernization of the storage architecture of Db2 Warehouse, a traditional full feature and high-performance database system with 3 decades of development, to exploit the new paradigm of cost-effective storage for the cloud. We discuss a solution based on the integration of LSM trees as part of the storage subsystem, that enables Db2 Warehouse to efficiently store data pages within object storage, and through the application of special techniques to minimize read and write latencies as well as all of the amplification factors (write, read, and storage), achieve not only storage cost savings, but also higher performance. Further, by retaining the traditional data page format, we are able to avoid significantly re-architecting the database kernel and thereby retain the substantial capabilities and optimizations of the existing system.
items.abstract_zh: 基于传统存储子系统构建的数据库系统通常将数据存储在称为数据页的小块中（由于历史原因，大小通常为 4KB 的倍数）。这些传统的存储子系统，例如网络附加块存储，是为块级别的高效随机访问 I/O 模式而设计的，并且块大小通常可由应用程序根据其需要进行配置。对于云环境中的大规模分析数据库，与云对象存储相比，这些传统存储子系统的成本效益不高，并且利用它们的数据库系统面临失去竞争力的风险。本文介绍了 Db2 Warehouse（经过 3 年发展的传统全功能高性能数据库系统）存储架构的现代化，以开发经济高效的云存储新范式。我们讨论一种基于将 LSM 树集成为存储子系统一部分的解决方案，该解决方案使 Db2 Warehouse 能够在对象存储中高效存储数据页，并通过应用特殊技术来最大限度地减少读写延迟以及所有放大因素（写入、读取和存储），不仅实现存储成本节省，而且实现更高的性能。此外，通过保留传统的数据页格式，我们能够避免对数据库内核进行重大的重新架构，从而保留现有系统的大量功能和优化。

items.title: RobOpt: A Tool for Robust Workload Optimization Based on Uncertainty-Aware Machine Learning.
items.abstract: Relational database management systems (RDBMSs) employ query optimizers to search for execution plans deemed optimal for specific queries. Classical optimizers rely on inaccurate parameter estimates and assumptions that may not hold true in real-world scenarios. Consequently, suboptimal execution plans may be chosen, leading to poor query execution performance. Recent proposals of learned query optimizers that leverage Machine Learning suffer also from the selection of suboptimal plans. In order to fill this gap, we have created Robust Workload Optimization (RobOpt), a prototype tool that facilitates robust execution of a query workload in RDBMSs. It implements a novel technique that takes workload logs as input, generates training samples, and trains a risk-aware learned cost model. It optimizes risk-aware plan selection strategies to achieve a desired level of runtime performance and robustness. In addition, it analyzes a workload according to its training samples and determines an optimal plan selection strategy either at the workload or query level. Ultimately, it enables the robust execution of any workload by determining an optimal plan selection strategy per query. RobOpt can work on top of any RDBMS.
items.abstract_zh: 关系数据库管理系统 (RDBMS) 使用查询优化器来搜索对于特定查询而言最佳的执行计划。经典优化器依赖于不准确的参数估计和假设，这些估计和假设在现实场景中可能不成立。因此，可能会选择次优的执行计划，从而导致查询执行性能不佳。最近提出的利用机器学习的学习查询优化器也受到次优计划选择的影响。为了填补这一空白，我们创建了鲁棒工作负载优化 (RobOpt)，这是一个原型工具，可促进 RDBMS 中查询工作负载的鲁棒执行。它实现了一种新技术，将工作负载日志作为输入，生成训练样本，并训练具有风险意识的学习成本模型。它优化风险意识计划选择策略，以实现所需的运行时性能和稳健性水平。此外，它还根据训练样本分析工作负载，并在工作负载或查询级别确定最佳计划选择策略。最终，它通过确定每个查询的最佳计划选择策略来实现任何工作负载的稳健执行。 RobOpt 可以在任何 RDBMS 之上工作。

items.title: Apache Arrow DataFusion: A Fast, Embeddable, Modular Analytic Query Engine.
items.abstract:
Apache Arrow DataFusion is a fast, embeddable, and extensible query engine written in Rust that uses Apache Arrow as its memory model. In this paper we describe the technologies on which it is built, and how it fits in long-term database implementation trends. We then enumerate its features, optimizations, architecture and extension APIs to illustrate the breadth of requirements of modern OLAP engines as well as the interfaces needed by systems built with them. Finally, we demonstrate open standards and extensible design do not preclude state-of-the-art performance using a series of experimental comparisons to DuckDB.

While the individual techniques used in DataFusion have been previously described many times, it differs from other industrial strength engines by providing competitive performance and an open architecture that can be customized using more than 10 major extension APIs. This flexibility has led to use in many commercial and open source databases, machine learning pipelines, and other data-intensive systems. We anticipate that the accessibility and versatility of DataFusion, along with its competitive performance, will further the proliferation of high-performance custom data infrastructures tailored to specific needs assembled from modular components.
